programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Return of the American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service. Gibbs and McGee travel to Russia to escort a computer expert connected to classified intel. However, the mission is compromised and they are forced to go off the grid while being hunted by Russian mercenaries. Meanwhile, the team in DC frantically tries to locate the missing pair. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy Lieutenant who has been murdered while on his way to a meeting with the President, and must ascertain whether the killing was part of a recent spate of muggings in the area - or a targeted hit to keep information secret. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky works on a case involving his estranged childhood best friend, travelling to London with Special Agent Bishop to interview his pal's family and co-workers. While away, the detective reflects on his own past choices, and examines how they have affected his present life. Alice Krige guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. The trail appears to lead to a friend of Sofia's, another scientist who worked for Russia and who may have succumbed to professional jealousy. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on a joint Coast Guard and NCIS training exercise at sea with Agent Borin, Gibbs spots a dead man overboard, who is found to be a missing member of the crew of a nearby vessel. However, when the agent boards the ship for questioning, he realises it has been commandeered by pirates. Naval drama, starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team accepts a job repairing a turbine on a wind farm, but things take a critical turn when Paige and Happy are stranded in a faulty gondola that could plummet at any moment

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter and the team defy the orders of a foreign government as they try to retrieve an asteroid, which is thought to containing DNA strands that could prove deadly if exposed

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to track down wanted terrorist Harper Dearing before another US Navy ship is destroyed, and a desperate Gibbs enlists the aid of a prisoner to draw his target out of hiding. Meanwhile, Palmer is forced to consider putting his nuptials on hold as the stand-off with Dearing becomes heated. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate the death of an armoury employee when divers find her body in a car at the bottom of a lake. The victim had been reported missing since she was evacuated before the blast at NCIS headquarters. Meanwhile, crisis counsellor Miles Wolf carries out psychological assessments of all the agents in the wake of the bombings. Guest starring Steve Valentine (Crossing Jordan)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on medical leave, Ducky reopens a 12-year-old case by talking officials at a cemetery into releasing the body of a navy commander whose post-mortem he carried out 12 years previously. Meanwhile, the team is called in to investigate the death of a sergeant from carbon monoxide poisoning - and finds a copy of Ducky's exhumation request on the man's laptop. Crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the deaths of a radio DJ and a naval officer who were both killed during a live broadcast, only to discover there are a number of potential suspects. Evidence leads the agents to suspect the involvement of a radical group called Military at Home, prompting Tony to investigate an upmarket gated community - where his door-to-door inquiries yield an enticing proposition or two from the female residents. American drama, starring Michael Weatherly, with Nadia Bjorlin

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby becomes personally involved in the case of a murdered naval scientist who she regards as a kindred spirit. The team's initial inquiries turn up little concrete information about the victim's life, but the forensic specialist makes a breakthrough when she decodes some of the mysterious markings covering the body. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team members are surprised when Gibbs asks DiNozzo's father to join them in their investigation of a murder committed at the US Naval Academy. However, Tony Sr's experience turns out to play a vital role in the inquiry, which finds the team members hunting for a missing nuclear weapon they suspect is about to be sold on the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ziva confronts her past when the team is ordered to protect her father, Mossad director Eli David, at a gathering of former heads of NCIS. The agents discover he is being targeted by terrorists who have hijacked a shipment of advanced weapons from the army. Drama, guest starring TJ Ramini and Michael Nouri, with Cote de Pablo and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. The safe house is attacked, leaving Vance in hospital and Eli missing, with his bodyguard Amit Hadar dead. The movements of all the Palestinians are accounted for at the time of the attack, making it unclear who was responsible, and Ziva suspects her Mossad replacement Liat is hiding something. Drama, starring Michael Nouri, Cote de Pablo, Rocky Carroll and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A woman is shot dead in an apartment while keeping watch on a man on a yacht in a marina, and the killer takes her camera and computer. A flash drive is recovered from the victim's baseball cap and surveillance footage on it includes images of Callen, who deduces that the pictures were taken in the days leading up to the attempt on his life and that the dead woman was the last person to see him before he was shot

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam meet up with former colleague Mike Renko, who is back in town on the trail of a civilian militia he thinks is responsible for the theft of anti-tank missiles from a Navy base. Their discussion is interrupted by Hetty with footage of a man being strangled at an aquarium - and the victim is identified as a lance corporal who was Renko's inside man at the group

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate the murder of an engineer working on highly sensitive submarine technology, but find the crime scene clean of evidence. Forensic scientist Abby Sciuto travels from the NCIS office in Washington, DC to help out as the case has similarities to a serial killer she has been tracking. Guest starring Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate when former marine Asher Ross is killed by an explosive device hidden in his mobile phone. Because of the sophisticated nature of the bomb, suspicion falls on the members of the ordnance disposal unit Ross was part of in Iraq, but further enquiries reveal that they are being targeted as well

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A taxi driver and an admiral working at the Pentagon are found executed by the side of a road, but the agents have trouble ascertaining a motive - until they uncover links to a woman searching for her missing husband, a political refugee from Africa. Meanwhile, McGee makes a discovery about his new girlfriend. Starring Sean Murray, Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: CD investigates the death of a friend's son despite his demise being regarded as an accident, while Walker and Trivette track an escaped prisoner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. Henri Lubatti guest stars

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's heating breaks down, leaving them with no one to turn to - except their man-hungry neighbour Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe becomes head of the Public Duties Squad which supplies services to the private sector, leaving Gary and Margaret in charge of the restaurant. Richard Griffiths stars

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Unwilling copper Crabbe and his team are assigned to guard the weary jurors of a long-running trial. Being holed up in the hotel from hell does nothing to improve the gourmet policeman's temper, and evidence of some serious jury-rigging only makes matters worse. Meanwhile, back at the restaurant, Sally is harassed by a finicky diner. Richard Griffiths stars

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Obadiah Hakeswill returns to create more mischief, kidnapping a beautiful aristocrat and undermining the discipline of Wellesley's men. Sharpe is dispatched to thwart his old foe, and manages to inflict a humiliating defeat on French forces in the process - but only at terrible personal loss to himself. Sean Bean, Elizabeth Hurley and Pete Postlethwaite star

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A charity fundraiser who has travelled the country in a bath of baked beans is shot in a robbery, but his obsessive compulsive disorder gets on Gene's nerves, causing the DCI to beat a confession out of anyone who gets in his way. After the Clown warns Alex someone is going to die, she worries about Hunt's strong-arm tactics - and fights to keep control of the case. Matthew Macfadyen guest stars

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Alex realises there are just 24 hours until her parents die in a car bombing and chooses a horrified Ray to help her save them. Amid the chaos of a Gay Pride march, she arrests Tim and Caroline in the hope of keeping them safe, but with Lord Scarman due to visit the station a nervous Gene releases all the prisoners, aiming to make a good impression. Guest starring Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A young woman's body is found in a seemingly idyllic Northumberland coastal village in 1966, and the subsequent investigation leads Gently and Bacchus to suspect the victim's estranged husband is responsible for the killing. However, they soon come to realise her disturbed family is hiding an even more shocking secret. Starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Daniel Casey (Midsomer Murders), Shaun Dooley (Married Single Other), Paul Kaye (Two Thousand Acres of Sky) and Greg Hemphill (Still Game)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A bride-to-be and professional tennis player is found dead after her hen party, and the chief bridesmaid, best man and groom-to-be all come under the team's suspicion

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The new 'talkies' films are all the rage, meaning production of the exotic but silent Bride of Babylon is already in jeopardy - even before its lead actor is murdered on set. With one of Phryne's oldest friends producing the movie, she feels obliged to help identify the killer. Drama, starring Essie Davis

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki is humiliated in court when a barrister accuses her of making a serious error, while the teams investigates the death of a medical researcher. With Art Malik.

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A woman's severed leg is found in the grass at the side of a motorway and Taggart and Livingstone are brought into the investigation. As the body parts build up Taggart finds himself no closer to the solution and the investigation leads them to a butcher who becomes a prime suspect. However, when he turns up dead, killed with the same gun as the dismembered corpse, it appears to be another dead end

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh offends everyone in Whyhope as he desperately tries to hide his secret past, and Meryl launches a charm offensive to help Charlie deal with her pregnancy grief

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh and Penny are forced together during an overnight trip to a coal mine, and Meryl turns failure into success when she unveils a new statue to commemorate the town

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Diane encounters an old army colleague and the incident stirs painful memories of her service days when a young soldier committed suicide. Smithy lends a sympathetic ear when she confides in him about her guilty feelings at having failed to halt the bullying which caused the tragedy. Mickey pushes the boat out to capture Mia's heart and Honey invites Will back to her place for the night after an evening in the pub

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Turkish man reports his 16-year-old daughter missing, but further inquiries reveal she is happily living with her boyfriend and has no intention of returning to a life of drudgery and exploitation. Stuart takes over the case when the worried father denounces the boy's family for setting up illegal marriages for immigrants in return for cash. Mickey's old flame Carrie Morgan turns up at Sun Hill accused of theft by her employer, and the harassed detective realises he must distance himself from the ex-prostitute if he wants to make his relationship with Mia work

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: John Heaton tells Gina the station faces the threat of closure and the team must pull together to keep Sun Hill on the map. New arrival Sgt Nikki Wright (Gillian Taylforth) is determined to show what she's made of, but fails to impress Roger, who complains to Doug Wright about her, not realising they are a married couple. Tony investigates an armed robbery at a jeweller's and a drug addict's body is discovered in a disused flat. A proposal from Rod Jessop stuns June

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Dan attempts heroics in a bid to rescue an 11-year-old from a canal boat where he is being held. The abductor turns out to be Rick Harris, whose addict son was found dead the previous day. June's private life gets entangled with public duties when she's called to boyfriend Rod's school to investigate one of his pupils - Kyle Henderson of the troublesome lottery-winning family - and there's a shock in store for her when the headmaster is critically injured in a hit-and-run incident

programme.title: David Copperfield
programme.desc: The rural idyll of eight-year-old David Copperfield is brought to an abrupt end when his widowed mother Clara remarries. Young David takes an instant dislike to Mr Murdstone, his new step-father, and the newcomer proves the feeling is more than mutual when he send the lad away to a boarding school run by a ruthless headmaster. Adaptation of the Dickens classic with an all-star cast, including Pauline Quirke, Maggie Smith and Ian McKellen, with a young Daniel Radcliffe starring in the title role

programme.title: David Copperfield
programme.desc: David is sent to a workhouse by his evil stepfather following his mother's death. But he finds an unlikely ally in the eternally optimistic gambler Mr Micawber. Ciaran McMenamin, Bob Hoskins, Trevor Eve and Maggie Smith star in the second part of the Dickens tale

programme.title: David Copperfield
programme.desc: Now an ambitious young man making a name for himself in London, David returns to Suffolk with his schoolfriend Steerpike in tow to catch up with Peggoty, his late mother's maid. Adaptation of the Dickens classic, starring Pauline Quirke and Ciaran McMenamin

programme.title: David Copperfield
programme.desc: Micawber threatens to expose Uriah Heep for the devious character that he is, and David returns to Canterbury once again. Adaptation of the Dickens classic with an all-star cast, including Pauline Quirke, Maggie Smith, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Bob Hoskins

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Wingless Bird
programme.desc: Feature-length melodrama set in 1913, about the romantic relationship between Agnes Conway, whose parents own a sweetshop, and Charles Farrier, son of an altogether grander family. Agnes faces an added dilemma when she finds her heart divided between Charles and his soldier brother Reginald. Starring Claire Skinner and Edward Atterton

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is gripped by an outbreak of dysentery, but that's the least of mum-to-be Marie Amos's worries when her husband Tony is arrested for gross indecency in the gentlemen's toilets and faces a criminal hearing. Nurse Crane meets Dolores McEvoy, a pregnant Irish woman who is clearly malnourished - but when she visits the woman's home, it turns out she has given a false address and has disappeared. Trixie is delighted when Tom asks her to oversee her first official church duty since their engagement, while at Nonnatus House, Fred tackles a rat infestation - only for Sister Monica Joan to work against him. With guest appearances by Cara Theobold (Downton Abbey) and Richard Fleeshman (Coronation Street)

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Jean Valjean is tormented by his conscience and, under relentless pressure from Javert, is forced to confess his true identity. However, his life looks is changed entirely once again due to a promise he makes to Fantine to keep Cosette safe. The Thenardiers see a bright future ahead when a wealthy stranger seeks lodgings at their inn - but their joy is doomed to be short-lived. Javert rises to become a police officer in Paris, and pursues a new lead involving a report of a missing girl that could finally allow him to bring Valjean down

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Cromwell manoeuvres a bill through Parliament acknowledging Henry as head of the Church of England, the first step in enabling the king to grant his own divorce from Katherine of Aragon. The Boleyn family also looks to the lawyer to deal with Harry Percy, whose wife claims her own marriage is unlawful on the grounds that her husband had previously made a binding contract of marriage with Anne Boleyn. This is a task Cromwell approaches with relish, remembering how Percy helped bring down Wolsey. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis and Claire Foy

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: New eye-witness testimony and a television documentary about the McCready case casts doubt on the original murder investigation. DS Walker is suspended from duty and decides to return to Glasgow to find new evidence to prove his innocence, in order to save his career, relationship and reputation

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Jean Valjean is tormented by his conscience and, under relentless pressure from Javert, is forced to confess his true identity. However, his life looks is changed entirely once again due to a promise he makes to Fantine to keep Cosette safe. The Thenardiers see a bright future ahead when a wealthy stranger seeks lodgings at their inn - but their joy is doomed to be short-lived. Javert rises to become a police officer in Paris, and pursues a new lead involving a report of a missing girl that could finally allow him to bring Valjean down

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh decides to tell Ajax the truth about their relationship, against his family's advice, but it does not go as planned

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As the bills pile up, Tracey has to consider selling Darryl's car, while Sharon goes into the perfume business to raise some cash. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey and Sharon witness a robbery committed by someone they know, leaving them agonising over whether to turn a blind eye or inform on a mate. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey's house becomes infested with mice, forcing her to shelve plans for a perfume-selling party. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The team investigates an arson attack on the home of larger-than-life lottery-winners the Hendersons and Stuart pursues a link with the hit-and-run incident that put Rod Jessop in hospital. Forensics reveal it is the third in a series of fires and the pressure is on to find the culprit before someone gets killed. There's good news for Sam, and June is so relieved to see Rod on the road to recovery she accepts his proposal

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Mark calls the police when Daisha goes missing, but as they focus inquiries on him, he begins to fear the worst. Faye tells Joseph their relationship is finished, but Elliot tries to intervene. Michael is still discovering how working with Annalese will affect his personal and professional lives, and Linden realises he has become too involved with Callie and her problems

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie's son Louis responds to treatment and his grandad Kenneth has a Christmas surprise for the whole family. After finding out she is a diabetic, Bex drowns her sorrows in wine and cigarettes, unaware of the danger she is putting herself in. Jim whisks Nikki away for a cosy weekend at his remote country house, but the candlelit evening is ruined when Andy turns up

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CCTV footage proves Jay's girlfriend Cheryl was guilty of torching the home of troublesome lottery winners the Henderson family, but the team wonder whether the Sun Hill arsonist is still on the loose. Kezia sees a softer side to Smithy as he calmly solves a heated dispute and Lewis dreads living on his own when his gran announces she's going back to Jamaica. Sam tells ex-lovers Stuart and Phil she is to be their new boss

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ruth is annoyed with Mark over his gambling, Pat consults a solicitor, and Grant is annoyed Sam is hanging round with Tiffany

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pauline and Arthur have a misunderstanding about what to do with money given by Mark. Bianca's world is falling apart over David's continuing friendship with Sam.

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: An elderly Sarah reveals the truth about Bert Ford's death. Meanwhile in 1953, Sarah and Anna start their trial periods in their new homes, and Jack is furious at Carolyn for having kept their daughter a secret. Olivia becomes concerned over James's brutal hospital treatment and Elizabeth makes a shock announcement. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Part one of two. A lonely patient develops a crush on Andrew after he helps her overcome an operation that went wrong, but things turn nasty when her feelings aren't reciprocated. Meanwhile, Carol also finds herself in a quandary as it becomes clear that her new male friend has an unorthodox method of pain relief. Rural drama, starring Gary Mavers and Deborah Grant

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A local rascal goes on the straight and narrow to pay for his dog's veterinary bills, and Tristan has designs on Debbie Mount - but her father does not approve. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester falls out with the mayor, after attempting to discipline his daughter, and tries to explain the concept of fair play by referring to the rules of cricket. Meanwhile, William is having problems with their own daughter, Emma. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin ropes the reluctant Yellowcoats into entering the town carnival, and puts Spike in charge of designing their float. Jimmy Perry and David Croft's holiday camp comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth's wealthy acquaintance Mrs Fortescue, whose sister married a baronet, asks her for a lift into town - but things do not go according to plan. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: As Barry tires of golf and looks for a new hobby, Billy's latest idea of an archery course is right on target. Long-running comedy series, starring Mike Grady, Keith Clifford and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to investigate why newly planted roses at an upmarket preparatory school keep dying. After witnessing the arcane ritual of the Night of the Stag, they detect tensions between modernising headmaster Quentin Marshall and established members of his staff who are harbouring secrets someone is prepared to kill for. Drama, guest starring Anthony Andrews (Brideshead Revisited), with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Supermodel Lula Landry falls to her death from a Mayfair balcony and the police conclude that she committed suicide, but Lula's adoptive brother John Bristow believes she was murdered and that key evidence has been overlooked. He calls upon private investigator Cormoran Strike to examine the death further and the penniless and effectively homeless PI takes the case. Crime thriller based on books written by JK Rowling under the pseudonym Robert Galbraith, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gerry is spooked by the discovery of UCOS's latest victim - a cop, whose body has lain untouched under a basement for 30 years. As accusations of police corruption and underhand dealings begin to surface, Gerry has to face up to some skeletons in his own closet and it's a race against time for the team to help before his murky past catches up with him. While babysitting his grandson, Gerry becomes convinced that only he holds the key to the investigation and goes Awol to uncover the truth. Starring Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A group of environmental terrorists seize control of the Thames Barrier, threatening to flood London unless the Government release a secret document - one with far-reaching implications for the future of the planet. Adam and Ros try to diffuse the situation while a counter-offensive is planned, but end up putting their own lives in danger. Guest starring Cheryl Campbell and Anthony Flanagan

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CCTV footage proves Jay's girlfriend Cheryl was guilty of torching the home of troublesome lottery winners the Henderson family, but the team wonder whether the Sun Hill arsonist is still on the loose. programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge. programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge. programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: David Dimbleby presents live coverage of the ceremony in Whitehall commemorating those who died in war, which is this year a closed event due to the coronavirus pandemic. The two-minute silence at 11am will be followed by wreath laying at the foot of the Cenotaph as the nation remembers all those who died serving their country

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Lewes high street in East Sussex, as experts Philip Serrell and Nick Hall assist two teams each afforded an hour to buy three items in the hope that they will make the greater profit at auction. Anita also finds time to meet a local historian to discover the hidden past of nearby Worthing, before the winners are determined in the saleroom

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones marks Remembrance Sunday by visiting the Brecon Beacons, where the armed forces have trained since the Second World War. James Lusted meets an army veteran who finds peace in faith and music

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents today's FA Cup first-round results, along with reports and reaction from the afternoon's big games

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a new home in South Yorkshire with room enough for their children and any future grandchildren, with a budget of £450,000. Away from the property search, Alistair meets a national sled dog champion and takes the reins on a high-octane race through the woods

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to design duo Josh and Oli to repurpose an old mower, while upholsterer Leigh-Anne Treadwell goes out on a limb with her redesign of a pair of teak chairs. Sarah's challenge is to find new homes for three Persian rugs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Rich brothers David and Harry together with Arit Anderson travel to the West Midlands to help Richard and Darren create their very own slice of New York's 'High Line' garden in Solihull. The New York garden has famously turned an old disused, overground train line into a thriving garden that mixes contemporary paving, grass planting and historical, leftover train tracks. The couple want this garden to remind them of their honeymoon in the Big Apple

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith visit Cornwall, heading to Falmouth for the start of the oyster season, where successful hatching is down to the moon. They also discover how old traditions are being used in modern ways, from boatbuilding to using horses to manage an ancient landscape, while Charlotte helps out in the exotic gardens of Trelissick. Adam Henson finds out why British turkey farmers could be in for a tough time this Christmas.

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide who will be the first pair to leave the contest. With music by the Kanneh-Masons, and the Strictly professionals pay a Remembrance Day tribute to Vera Lynn

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Following Lord Asriel's tear through the worlds, Lyra and Pan journey to the strangely deserted city of Cittagazze, where they encounter Will - and reluctantly team up with the daemonless boy. Mrs Coulter interrogates a suspected heretic, while at the witch council, Lee Scoresby reveals he is seeking out an explorer who could offer Lyra protection. The return of the adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Amir Wilson and Ruth Wilson

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: As Dawn Ellson's premiership falters, Peter makes a move for the top spot - but with his enemies circling, he is forced to make a final roll of the dice. Thriller by David Hare, starring Hugh Laurie and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester City v Liverpool at Etihad Stadium and Leicester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Molineux. The Citizens overcame Liverpool 4-0 last term, but the Reds had already claimed the league title Pep Guardiola's men had won in the previous two seasons. Both meetings between the Foxes and Wolves finished goalless last season, but both sides will have been hopeful of breaking the deadlock on this occasion having made steady starts to their respective campaigns

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester United v Arsenal, Chelsea v Everton and Manchester City v Bristol City

programme.title: Children in Need: 40 Fabulous Years
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan takes viewers on a funny, poignant and revealing tour across four decades of Children in Need

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a stone house in Abercynon, South Wales, a two-bed semi in West Bromwich, and a first-floor maisonette in Neasden, north-west London

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty returns with the programme exposing cases of fraud. Insurers combine forces to catch a serial fraudster making fake claims off the back of high-profile tragedies, while digital data reveals that a claim for £35,000 worth of stolen jewellery is a complete lie

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A Scottish cyclist on a round the world challenge is hit by a driver in Texas, and a family dog saves the day when a woman slips and falls on the stairs. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Danny Sebastian help two teams look for items in Shrewsbury, while Natasha Raskin Sharp explores an incredible museum and meets the collector behind it

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid has a difficult conversation with Deborah, while Zara's obsession with Harper gets out of hand. Valerie struggles to help a terminally ill woman reconnect with her estranged daughter

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: The return of the programme featuring the stories of people who bought properties they were introduced to when they appeared on Escape to the Country. Sonali Shah visits a couple who bought one of the houses presented to them on a 2019 property hunt in Bedfordshire, then meets a couple who have put down roots together in the Herefordshire countryside after renovating a property from the 1700s

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, then she and two experts transform a dressing table, a vintage suitcase and a petite sofa

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which experts travel around the UK in search of bargains before heading to an auction to find out whose haul proves most popular with buyers. In the first edition, Charles Hanson and James Braxton setting out in a 1959 MG to look for items in Oxfordshire

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present a special week of shows following the Rickshaw Challenge for Children in Need, which taking place in the grounds of the Goodwood Estate. Matt Baker provides live updates while riding alongside Team Rickshaw, and celebrity guests will be in the studio to help cheer them on

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin finds out how Liverpool coped with restrictions due to Covid-19 infection rates, hearing from residents worried about the second wave, business owners pushed to the brink of despair, and those who say that living under tier three rules just isn't possible

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick is shocked to come face to face with his ex-care worker Katy Lewis and immediately demands to know why she never told him that Frankie was his daughter - but he is stunned at her response. Lola opens up to Honey about her relationship troubles with Jay

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Countdown dictionary expert Susie Dent, comedian Bob Mortimer and TV presenters Ore Oduba and Stacey Solomon join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The barrister helps the second and third generations of British Jewish families affected by the Holocaust to retrace their relatives' footsteps and discover the full truth about what happened to them. Robert begins by meeting two British Jewish families who want to investigate what happened to their relatives, as well as exploring his own family's stories on both his mother's and his father's sides

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following life at Newcastle hair salon Longlox. Sammyjo makes a shock announcement, while there are potential promotions for the juniors and a mishap at Ashleigh's housewarming party

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The staff get used to two new colleagues at the salon, while Sammyjo surprises herself with a new love interest and has a decision to make about the junior promotions

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Roisin Conaty and Armando Iannucci joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before welcoming another group of Hollywood A-listers and British TV big shots to chat about what they've been up to recently. This week he's joined by pop queen Kylie Minogue, who chats and performs her current single Magic, British boxing champion-turned-Strictly contestant Nicola Adams, and actors Josh O'Connor and Emma Corrin, who are playing the roles of Charles and Diana in the new season of The Crown

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Featuring a group of dogs riding Russia's underground rail system, and a clever crow that can use a pedestrian crossing to crack a tough problem

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his friends explore Cambodia's Angkor Wat Temple, the biggest religious monument in the world, and dance the macarena at a mythological theme park in Singapore

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The team are out in the BP garden celebrating Bonfire Night this week. Lindsey Russell, Richie Driss, Mwaksy Mudenda and Adam Beales are cooking and toasting marshmallows over an open fire, they launch the BP Book Awards and there is a sneak peek behind the scenes of series two of BBC One hit drama His Dark Materials

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia reveals a harrowing past and Poppy takes over the show

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia and her friends hold the series wrap party in the flat

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to unearth the dinosaurs that were far deadlier than they first appeared. He discovers how the Sinornithosaurus hides a deadly dental secret, races a Velociraptor in a climbing challenge and puts Argentinasaurus to the ultimate test

programme.title: The Big Life Fix with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Designer and technologist Haiyan Zhang tries to come up with a monitoring solution for the parents of four-year-old William, whose rare form of epilepsy means they have to watch him around the clock, leaving them exhausted. Ian, 19, who has cerebral palsy, wants to find a way of taking control of a sailing boat, and 66-year-old multiple sclerosis sufferer Susan needs help operating the items in her home

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles in the arena, including a robot designed by a nine-year-old girl, which comes complete with a huge pink axe. Other entrants include a team of students from Falkirk whose robot is made of wood and has a petrol engine, and a family team with an ingenious creation made up of parts sourced from their local scrapyard

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham features animals that utilise devious tactics to win a mate and raise a family, such as bower birds and fiddler crabs, who use optical illusions to impress a female, and male topi antelope, who fake danger to give them an edge. He also looks at family life, including the chicks that blackmail their parents, and the honeyguide bird that dupes another species into raising its young

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary histories of some of Britain's many islands, making a tempura dish from queen scallops caught off the Isle of Man and a rich cranachan with whisky from Islay. They also travel to the Isle of Wight to enjoy the cooking of a Michelin-starred chef and sample a Jersey shellfish delicacy

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Meerkat pups Squirt and Weeny belong to one of the most famous animal families on the planet and are also part of a 20-year animal behaviour study. David Attenborough narrates the full story of the research, from the early days when it was a challenge to even get close to a meerkat to the latest revelations about these animals and their complex lives

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len Goodman takes his dancing pal Anton du Beke back to Blackpool to reminisce about a seaside holiday in 1981, including taking him for a tide on a vintage tram

programme.title: On the Town (1949)
programme.desc: Musical following three sailors who are out for a good time while on a day's shore leave in New York. One tries initially to fend off the advances of a female taxi driver, another is pulled into helping with an anthropologist's study of prehistoric man, while the third seeks out the model in a famous advertising campaign - but finds she is not quite what he expected. With Gene Kelly, Frank Sinatra and Jules Munshin

programme.title: Ingenious Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts explore creatures' surprising physical characteristics, including how one man's understanding of an eagle's physical abilities could help save it from extinction. Conservationist Giles Clark witnesses an Australian kangaroo that is more at home climbing trees than hopping through the Outback, zoologist Lucy Cooke is in Costa Rica to find out if the sloth's laziness is the key to its success, and marine biologist Patrick Aryee reveals the secrets of one of the world's oddest looking creatures - the platypus

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: FC United of Manchester v Doncaster Rovers (Kick-off 5.30pm). Dan Walker presents all the action from the FA Cup first-round tie at Broadhurst Park. The non-league hosts reached this stage after defeating Guisleley in the final qualifying round and defeated Football League opposition at this stage of the 2010/11 competition when they won 3-2 away to Rochdale. However, they will still go into this match as underdogs against the League One outfit

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: In the final episode, Jamie Crawford takes to the skies, using aerial images to find out how the Scottish people have harnessed our precious natural resources to power our industry, from a remote loch near Ullapool to the Carron iron works of Falkirk. He also travels to Shetland to follow in the footsteps of his father, who went there to work during the oil industry boom

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Doctor McCeavedy accidentally discovers that Frazer keeps a hoard of gold sovereigns in his home and Mainwaring tries to persuade the undertaker that his life savings would be better kept in a safer place - namely his bank. Vintage comedy, with Arthur Lowe and John Laurie. First shown in 1977

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of reflections this week, with Joe Lycett, Zoe Lyons, Liza Tarbuck and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Perfect 10 (2019)
programme.desc: Fourteen-year-old Leigh lives on the outskirts of Brighton with her neglectful father. A talented gymnast, she is training hard for her first competition, despite a lack of confidence. But when an older half-brother she never knew existed turns up at the house one night, her lonely existence is altered, and turns her feelings on their head. Coming-of-age drama, starring Frankie Box, Alfie Deegan, Sharlene Whyte and William Ash

programme.title: Silence (2016)
programme.desc: In the 17th century, two Jesuit priests travel from Portugal to Edo-era Japan to locate their missing mentor and spread Catholic Christianity. Martin Scorsese's historical drama, starring Andrew Garfield, Adam Driver, Tadanobu Asano, Ciaran Hinds and Liam Neeson

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter O, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter P, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the sixth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to lead rambles and meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker is in the Peak District to take on challenge with 16-year-old Harvey, who was supported by bereavement counselling after his brother was killed, while Steve Brown is inspired to attempt scaling Wales' Pen y Fan mountain in his wheelchair after meeting 5-year-old Emerson, a sport-lover with a spinal-cord injury

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: To mark the Remembrance commemorations, Dougie Vipond is in Kingussie finding out about the lost soldiers of the Indian Army who never went home and Anne Lundon hears the story of three men from Ballachulish who managed to talk their way back to freedom from German-occupied France in 1940. Arlene finds out why broadcaster Lesley Riddoch is championing the hutting movement in Scotland and Euan McIlwraith discovers the complex geology of the Isle of Mull

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: A visit to two unique allotment initiatives that take growing together to a whole different level. First, Moncrieff Allotments in the middle of Perth, a multicultural allotment that brings a diverse group of people together to garden in the middle of the sometimes flooding, Tay. Next, the programme heads to Tillicoultry Allotments in the shadow of the beautiful Ochil hills, which looks like a series of beautifully interlinked back gardens

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Masters 2019: Tiger Roars Again
programme.desc: A look back at Tiger Woods' memorable victory at Augusta National Golf Club, which saw the American win this tournament for the fifth time in his career, and raise his overall tally of Major victories to 15. Woods is second only to Jack Nicklaus, who triumphed six times at this famous golf course, and finished with 18 Major titles to his name

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: News from the first round of the FA Cup

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes to keep children happy, including Paul Hollywood's sausage rolls, James Martin's banana split and Lorraine Pascale's pizza recipe

programme.title: Reel History of Britain
programme.desc: Melvyn Bragg and the vintage mobile cinema travel to Bristol Airport, where he explores the rising popularity of package holidays during the 1970s. Former entertainer Paul James recalls how Pontin's set up its first Mediterranean business, and Jean Pitt and Jane Hosegood discuss being travel reps

programme.title: I Was Monty's Double (1958)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about actor and army officer ME Clifton-James, whose remarkable resemblance to General Montgomery saw him selected for a special assignment during the Second World War. He was trained to imitate the general perfectly, and sent on a tour of North Africa to keep the real Montgomery's whereabouts unknown to the enemy. Clifton-James stars as both himself and Monty, alongside John Mills and Cecil Parker

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Silent Witness star Liz Carr traces her family tree, learning about the life of her maternal grandfather, who died when her mother was just two. She also discovers that her paternal great-great-great-grandfather was involved in the attempted murder of a prominent landlord in Armagh in the 1850s

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents highlights of the first-round ties in the world's oldest domestic cup football competition, including Chorley v Wigan Athletic at Victory Park, Tonbridge Angels v Bradford City at Longmead Stadium and Colchester United v Marine at JobServe Community Stadium

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: Highlights of the ceremony in Whitehall commemorating those who died in war, which is this year a closed event due to the coronavirus pandemic. The two-minute silence at 11am was followed by wreath laying at the foot of the Cenotaph as the nation remembers all those who died serving their country

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Simon Reeve travels through Cornwall as the county emerges from lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britain's poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: The Trials of Oscar Pistorius
programme.desc: Documentary series focusing on the South African former Paralympic star, who was convicted for the murder of his girlfriend Reeva Steenkamp on Valentine's Day in 2013

programme.title: They Shall Not Grow Old (2018)
programme.desc: Peter Jackson's documentary exploring the First World War, edited from rarely seen archive footage from the Western Front which has been restored and colourised, while lip readings of the men featured in the footage have allowed their speech to be reconstructed. The film also includes archive interviews with servicemen from the BBC and the Imperial War Museum

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter searches for answers about his true identity with Caitlin, who seeks to avenge her brother's death. Hiro finds himself in a difficult situation as he struggles to control his feelings for Yaeko, and West convinces Claire to stand up for herself. Milo Ventimiglia, Hayden Panettiere, Masi Oka and Katie Carr star

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter and Caitlin discover the shocking consequences of the Shanti virus as they teleport into the future. West is stunned to learn the identity of Claire's father, while a group of heroes confronts the Nightmare Man. Milo Ventimiglia, Hayden Panettiere and Katie Carr star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary talks to award-winning film-maker and Turner Prize-winning artist Steve McQueen, discussing his views on growing up in London and his new BBC One anthology series Small Axe. Set from the late-1960s to the mid-1980s, each film tells a different story involving racism and discrimination in London's West Indian community

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by comedians Jo Brand and Andi Osho, actor and comedian Adrian Edmondson and singer Will Young, who share their favourite books as well as revealing the books that remind them of a particular person and their recommended non-fiction reads. They all discuss this week's book group picks - This Lovely City by Louise Hare and Will's own book, To Be a Gay Man

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from the Britannia Panopticon Theatre in Glasgow, looking back at some memorable 'musical buys' of the past and along the way dropping in on some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers. Christina Trevanion also makes an appearance, paid a visit by Tim Weeks the gramophone guru. Plus, Anita Manning catches up with auctioneer Charles Hanson to learn more about singer and entertainer George Formby

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Peterson gets to grips with some old lampshades, while blacksmith Kev Paxton is tasked with transforming some piling metal. Presenter Jay Blades bags himself some windbreaker poles, but will all three projects result in a profit for the items' unsuspecting owners? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help Aliyah and Ajaz, who have two young children, making mealtimes hard work. To make matters worse, the couple have differing tastes, so they rarely eat the same meal. And when time is against them, the takeaway wins yet again. Can Gregg and Chris help change their costly ways? Plus, Chris visits a chilli farm in Devon to try the world's hottest chilli and a team of tennis players taste test five lemonades

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: David Cornock presents a round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from Thursday and Friday's Super League fixtures, including Catalans Dragons v St Helens and Castleford Tigers v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Nigel Planer back to Ottery St Mary in Devon, where the actor reminisces about a childhood holiday he had there in 1960

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Woody ropes George in to help him with a secret mission. Meanwhile, Tracy gets Katie to join the teaching staff. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites 10 home pottery enthusiasts to gather in Stoke-on-Trent, where they take part in a competitive throwing contest to determine which of them should be named Top Potter. Through a series of 18 challenges, taking place over the course of 35 days, the competitors will be judged on their craft skills and creativity by ceramic artist Kate Malone and master potter Keith Brymer Jones. The competition gets under way as the amateurs take part in a four-day assignment, in which they must produce a set of stackable kitchen bowls from lumps of earthenware clay. They also face a Spot Test, in which they must attach handles to 20 mugs within a strict time limit, and a Throw Down, where the judges ask them to make as many egg-cups as they can within 20 minutes

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Documentary following zookeeper Giles Clark as he hand-rears Sumatran tiger cubs Spot and Stripe, which were born at the Australia Zoo in Queensland and are one of the world's most critically endangered species. To help give them the best start and ensure their survival, Giles takes them home to live with his boisterous family and gives them round-the-clock care for the first four months of their lives. The film gives an intimate look into the crucial early development of the cubs as they struggle through their first crucial weeks, while Giles embarks upon the toughest parenting job of his life and tries to save the species from the brink of extinction in the wild. Andrew Lincoln narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Lewis are at Herstmonceux Castle in East Sussex, where a Chinese wall plaque catches James's eye, and Paul Martin explores the history of the building

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Ranj Singh and Meera Syal test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by the second couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend, and there are interviews with the remaining pairs

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Mark chapman presents coverage of the draw, as the remaining non-league and League One and Two clubs await their fate. Last season Maldon and Tiptree were drawn against Newport County, while fellow eighth-tier side Chichester drew Tranmere Rovers

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as sacred music fans Polyhymnians take on seaside dwellers the Sandy Shores. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of C for capture, E for effortlessness, T for tantalise, and U for utilise

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares the rhythms and rituals of her kitchen and reveals the inspiration behind many of her recipes. She begins with her take on bhorta, a dish of fried and mashed vegetables mainly associated with Bangladesh, to which she introduces a very British - but very familiar - ingredient. After creating a warm, spicy and aromatic dish of lamb shanks with wide noodles, Nigella uses very ripe bananas to make a rich and gooey chocolate tahini pudding, and then goes on to create a warming curry with a surprising ingredient

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces, this time focusing on the impact of the pandemic at the Royal Free London, a world leader in the treatment of infectious diseases and one of the biggest NHS trusts in the country. In the first edition, the UK lockdown is over, but hospital staff are counting the cost of having to prioritise Covid-19 above almost everything else as they battle to treat the patients left behind

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Comedy show hosted by Romesh Ranganathan in which 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain.. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Being Frank: The Frank Gardner Story
programme.desc: The BBC security correspondent explores what it is like to suddenly become disabled, something he experienced himself 16 years ago after being shot by Al-Qaeda gunmen in Saudi Arabia. The bullets damaged his spinal nerves, leaving him partially paralysed and he has used a wheelchair ever since. In this deeply personal film, he talks candidly about the effects his injuries have had on his life, work, relationships and the way he views himself. And he speaks to others, to explore how they have responded to sudden life-changing injuries

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the sixth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to lead rambles and meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker is in the Peak District to take on challenge with 16-year-old Harvey, who was supported by bereavement counselling after his brother was killed, while Steve Brown is inspired to attempt scaling Wales' Pen y Fan mountain in his wheelchair after meeting 5-year-old Emerson, a sport-lover with a spinal-cord injury

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in a casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: How groundbreaking research into the psychology of crowds could help prevent attacks on police. Plus, a report on a surge in coughing attacks on police officers

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Pipers of the Trenches
programme.desc: Around 2,500 pipers from Scotland, England, Ireland, Canada, Australia and New Zealand served in the First World War and within a year a thousand of them were dead. This film uncovers stories of bravery as descendants of those pipers visit the Somme and Gallipoli, the battlefields where their unarmed relatives accompanied their comrades over the top. Military historians provide insights into the courage of these men and the prospect they faced as they emerged from the trenches and led the troops into battle

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor and writer heads for Australia, where he helps a sick crocodile in the Northern Territory and encounters 'jackaroos' who round up camels in the desert around Alice Springs. After travelling to Sydney he plays a cameo role in soap Home and Away, before leaving for New Zealand, where he meets bungee jumpers and university freshmen in the Water of Leith

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Two men are brought in for questioning in Paris about the killing of Victoria and confess to having paid her to be the surrogate mother of their baby, while several leads take Rolf and Claire back to Poland. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Rolf, Claire and Karol do not get much help from the nuns as they try to find answers in a convent in Poland, while Rolf then discovers a connection to the case that points to a fertility clinic in Paris. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on March 1, with performances by artists including Shakin' Stevens, Electribe 101, Michael Bolton, Rod Stewart, Jamie J Morgan, Beats International featuring Lindy Layton and JT & the Big Family

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on March 8, with performances by artists including Bros, Guru Josh, Marc Almond, Gloria Estefan, JT & the Big Family, Innocence, Erasure, David A Stewart featuring Candy Dulfer, Bros, Beats International featuring Lindy Layton and the B-52's

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The captive Jeanette gives birth, and Freddie manages to locate her using a tracking device, but fails to arrive in time to find his missing baby. With resources now too scarce to adequately follow-up on the development, Saga is brought back from suspension to investigate a tip-off that Claes Sandberg is behind the abduction. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Rasmus discovers Freddie's abandoned helicopter in a disused concrete factory, and awaits the arrival of back-up. In Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor and writer heads for Australia, where he helps a sick crocodile in the Northern Territory and encounters 'jackaroos' who round up camels in the desert around Alice Springs. After travelling to Sydney he plays a cameo role in soap Home and Away, before leaving for New Zealand, where he meets bungee jumpers and university freshmen in the Water of Leith

programme.title: Pipers of the Trenches
programme.desc: Around 2,500 pipers from Scotland, England, Ireland, Canada, Australia and New Zealand served in the First World War and within a year a thousand of them were dead. This film uncovers stories of bravery as descendants of those pipers visit the Somme and Gallipoli, the battlefields where their unarmed relatives accompanied their comrades over the top. Military historians provide insights into the courage of these men and the prospect they faced as they emerged from the trenches and led the troops into battle

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores some of the most bizarre and extraordinary natural events on the planet. This episode visits the town brought to a standstill by a swarm of flying insects, meets the polar bears whose bizarre friendships shocked a community and the sleepless residents kept awake by a lover's deafening serenade

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: After being colonised by humans, Puerto Rico's rich natural resources were rapidly exploited for agriculture, industry and urban development. This documentary follows a conservation team fighting to save the most endangered creatures from extinction after they have been pushed to the edge of existence by human encroachment. David Attenborough narrates

programme.title: Dame Vera Lynn: We'll Meet Again
programme.desc: In a revised version of a documentary first shown in 2017 to mark the 100th birthday of the 'forces sweetheart', Katie Derham makes use of archive footage and family records to tell the story of how Vera Lynn became one of the UK's most well-known musical stars. Vera marked her centenary by reminiscing about her early days in showbusiness, with help from her daughter Virginia. She recalls starting out as Vera Welch in the 1920s, first appearing on stage at the tender age of seven, before charming the nation's servicemen during the Second World War. She also remembers becoming the first British artist to achieve a number one in America in the 1950s, and a similar feat in 2009, when she became the oldest living singer to reach the same position in the UK charts. Along the way, Second World War veterans and entertainment industry figures - including Paul McCartney, Barry Humphries and Miriam Margolyes - pay tribute to the power of her voice

programme.title: Dame Vera Lynn at the BBC
programme.desc: A look at some of Vera Lynn's favourite performances and biggest hits. As well as the many classic songs that helped unify the nation throughout the Second World War, this retrospective features upbeat song-and-dance performances from 1970s series The Vera Lynn Show. Plus, she joins forces with fellow stars like Harry Secombe, Des O'Connor and Bing Crosby

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Chris Lintott report on the discovery of phosphine gas in the clouds of Venus, which could be a sign of life. They also investigate the latest missions to Mars and the icy moons of Jupiter and Saturn. Plus, Pete Lawrence previews the best meteor showers of the year

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The scientists scouring the universe for signs of extraterrestrial life, including the film researchers at the Green Bank Telescope searching for radio signals from 'Tabby's Star', a star so mysterious some scientists believe it might be surrounded by a Dyson Sphere - a vast energy collector built by advanced aliens. The film also features the 2006 discovery by husband and wife team Duncan Lorimer and Maura McLaughlin of an enigmatic signal from space, known as a fast radio burst, and its subsequent explanations

programme.title: Barneys, Books and Bust Ups: 50 Years of the Booker Prize
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of the literary award to mark its 50th birthday, revealing how it belied humble beginnings to go on to revolutionise the world of literary fiction and become a central part of British cultural life. With contributions by Booker-winning authors Peter Carey, Penelope Lively and John Banville

programme.title: B Is for Book
programme.desc: Following a group of primary school children over the course of a year as they learn to read, with some making a flying start as others struggle to learn the alphabet. The film also reveals how much help they receive at home from their parents, some of whom only speak English as a foreign language

programme.title: Dame Vera Lynn: We'll Meet Again
programme.desc: In a revised version of a documentary first shown in 2017 to mark the 100th birthday of the 'forces sweetheart', Katie Derham makes use of archive footage and family records to tell the story of how Vera Lynn became one of the UK's most well-known musical stars. Vera marked her centenary by reminiscing about her early days in showbusiness, with help from her daughter Virginia. She recalls starting out as Vera Welch in the 1920s, first appearing on stage at the tender age of seven, before charming the nation's servicemen during the Second World War. She also remembers becoming the first British artist to achieve a number one in America in the 1950s, and a similar feat in 2009, when she became the oldest living singer to reach the same position in the UK charts. Along the way, Second World War veterans and entertainment industry figures - including Paul McCartney, Barry Humphries and Miriam Margolyes - pay tribute to the power of her voice

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures the beauty of the beach as the sun goes down, in a seascape painted in gold, purple and orange

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel gets cold feet as the wedding approaches, while Jean tries to remain calm despite her sister-in-law's interference. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould try to uncover the truth behind three mysterious portraits. The paintings in question are that of a child, thought to have been painted by modern artist Willem de Kooning, the image of a lady, considered the work of 18th-century society painter Philip Mercier, and a portrait of an old general, attributed to the 19th-century German master Adolph von Menzel. The investigation forces the duo to embark on a worldwide quest, taking them from Miami, Florida, to Berlin and a small town in Belgium

programme.title: 21st Century Mythologies with Richard Clay
programme.desc: The art historian explores some of the everyday myths of 21st-century popular culture, revealing the hidden meanings in everything from money, Wi-fi and race to the Madonna. Along the way, he meets avant-garde artists including Clet Abraham, Ingrid Burrington, Molly Soda and Rene Matic, and looks at semiotics, the study of signs and symbols that provides an analytical toolkit to helps navigate advertising and its demands for attention

programme.title: Berlin 1945
programme.desc: Life in the German capital in the year the Second World War ended, told through the stories of those who experienced it - from ordinary Germans to the Allied forces who entered the city. At the beginning of 1945, Berlin remains under the spell of the Nazi promise of salvation, an illusion at odds with the city's daily reality of bombing raids, fires to be extinguished and corpses to be buried. Life goes on as the front lines of the war close in each day

programme.title: A Scottish Soldier: A Lost Diary of WWI
programme.desc: Jackie Kay tells the story of black soldier Arthur Roberts, who grew up in Glasgow and joined the King's Own Scottish Borderers in 1917 and fought at Ypres

programme.title: Dame Vera Lynn at the BBC
programme.desc: A look at some of Vera Lynn's favourite performances and biggest hits. As well as the many classic songs that helped unify the nation throughout the Second World War, this retrospective features upbeat song-and-dance performances from 1970s series The Vera Lynn Show. Plus, she joins forces with fellow stars like Harry Secombe, Des O'Connor and Bing Crosby

programme.title: Hilary Mantel: Return to Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Profile of author Hilary Mantel in the six months leading up to the publication of The Mirror and the Light, the final novel in her award-winning Tudor trilogy telling the story of the extraordinary rise and sudden fall of Thomas Cromwell. Hilary reveals why she embarked on the writing of the trilogy and how it has changed her life. She also delves into her past and how she grew up with a dark family secret
