programme.desc: Peter hears about his father's greatest ever heist and embarks on some high-risk highway robbery, setting his sights on Mr McGregor's wheelbarrow of potatoes bound for the market

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster wants to wear his favourite hat on Favourite Hat Day, but it's falling apart!

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies' motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that go off-road

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy embarks on an epic adventure with a tiny grasshopper mouse

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: When Ranger Bevel gets stuck in a pile of soft snow on High Snowy Mountain, Bitz needs to work out how they can pull him out without sinking into it too

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle ends up in Evie's class, when he must be still so the headteacher doesn't see him.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo discovers that Gran Gran used to be in a band with Grandad and decides that she wants to learn how to play the drums. Gran Gran teaches JoJo how to play to a beat ,and eventually they form a new band with Jared and Cynthia called JoJo and the Jammers!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: A search for shade on a hot day leads Sarah and Duck to discover a fascinating tower in the park

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Pepi Nana is big and Little Nana is small and when the two friends go for a walk together, they discover how they see things differently - and how much fun that can be

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The froglets are playing chase with Tiny on her scooter, but Small can't run fast enough to keep up. So he makes his own space hopper; but unplanned, it hops him into orbit

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy teaches Igglepiggle how to kick a ball around the garden

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Nihal Arthanayake reads Dogs Don't Do Ballet, by Anna Kemp

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v Liverpool and Everton v Manchester United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blues visit Lincolnshire Antiques & Home Show, assisted by Nick Hall and Gary Pe. Presenter Charlie Ross gives his top tips for bagging a bargain at an antiques fair

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a new home in South Yorkshire with room enough for their children and any future grandchildren, with a budget of £450,000. Away from the property search, Alistair meets a national sled dog champion and takes the reins on a high-octane race through the woods

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway appreciates the scenery of Berwyn Mountains in north-east Wales on a walk in which the presenter is accompanied by a pair of llamas. Derek also visits Tenby, leaving the tourist hotspots behind to discover more about the town's hidden past

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: In tonight's live final just four bands remain, and they'll perform for Little Mix and the viewers at home, who will be able to vote for the winner. After an exciting journey, which band will come out on top and win the prize of a lifetime supporting Little Mix on their next UK tour?

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 11 couples return to the Strictly Ballroom for movie week, performing routines inspired by, or paying tribute to, Hollywood films. Not only do the Strictly stars have to impress the judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but they also have to impress the viewers at home

programme.title: Festival of Remembrance
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents the Royal British Legion's annual Festival of Remembrance, a programme that has been pre-recorded due to the Covid-19 pandemic, paying tribute to the those who lost their lives as victims of conflict, from the First World War to the present day

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge. Both league meetings between Everton and United ended 1-1 last term and another closely fought encounter was anticipated. The Blades came back from 2-0 down at Chelsea last term to rescue a point, but had struggled for form so far this term

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers

programme.title: Bobby (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge. Both league meetings between Everton and United ended 1-1 last term and another closely fought encounter was anticipated. The Blades came back from 2-0 down at Chelsea last term to rescue a point, but had struggled for form so far this term

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: David Dimbleby presents live coverage of the ceremony in Whitehall commemorating those who died in war, which is this year a closed event due to the coronavirus pandemic. The two-minute silence at 11am will be followed by wreath laying at the foot of the Cenotaph as the nation remembers all those who died serving their country

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Lewes high street in East Sussex, as experts Philip Serrell and Nick Hall assist two teams each afforded an hour to buy three items in the hope that they will make the greater profit at auction. Anita also finds time to meet a local historian to discover the hidden past of nearby Worthing, before the winners are determined in the saleroom

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones marks Remembrance Sunday by visiting the Brecon Beacons, where the armed forces have trained since the Second World War. James Lusted meets an army veteran who finds peace in faith and music

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents today's FA Cup first-round results, along with reports and reaction from the afternoon's big games

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to design duo Josh and Oli to repurpose an old mower, while upholsterer Leigh-Anne Treadwell goes out on a limb with her redesign of a pair of teak chairs. Sarah's challenge is to find new homes for three Persian rugs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Rich brothers David and Harry together with Arit Anderson travel to the West Midlands to help Richard and Darren create their very own slice of New York's 'High Line' garden in Solihull. The New York garden has famously turned an old disused, overground train line into a thriving garden that mixes contemporary paving, grass planting and historical, leftover train tracks. The couple want this garden to remind them of their honeymoon in the Big Apple

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary from the Our Lives strand following Ned, Sophie, Rowan and Sam Scharer as they take over as the wardens of Bardsey Island off the coast of off the Llyn Peninsula in Gwynedd, managing, renovating and caring for the island while hoping to become closer as a family in the process

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop
programme.desc: Samm helps a 60-something nurse who will retire and marry with just a few days in between. However, health difficulties may keep her from walking down the aisle unaided. Elsewhere, a younger bride's combination of big dreams and tight budget forces the team to get creative

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The families preparations for the final concert are added to their already exhausting workload and consider their experiences so far. Nicola Reynolds narrates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith visit Cornwall, heading to Falmouth for the start of the oyster season, where successful hatching is down to the moon. They also discover how old traditions are being used in modern ways, from boatbuilding to using horses to manage an ancient landscape, while Charlotte helps out in the exotic gardens of Trelissick. Adam Henson finds out why British turkey farmers could be in for a tough time this Christmas.

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide who will be the first pair to leave the contest. With music by the Kanneh-Masons, and the Strictly professionals pay a Remembrance Day tribute to Vera Lynn

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Following Lord Asriel's tear through the worlds, Lyra and Pan journey to the strangely deserted city of Cittagazze, where they encounter Will - and reluctantly team up with the daemonless boy. Mrs Coulter interrogates a suspected heretic, while at the witch council, Lee Scoresby reveals he is seeking out an explorer who could offer Lyra protection. The return of the adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Amir Wilson and Ruth Wilson

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: As Dawn Ellson's premiership falters, Peter makes a move for the top spot - but with his enemies circling, he is forced to make a final roll of the dice. Thriller by David Hare, starring Hugh Laurie and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: A quiet Cardiff community was devastated in 1959 by the abduction of six-year-old Carol Ann Stephens, last seen getting into a car with a mysterious, dark-haired stranger. A nationwide recovery effort was launched, but two weeks later, Carol Ann's body was found in a brook, 60 miles from where she'd been taken. Her killer was never caught. The team comes across new evidence that could finally put this case to rest

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester United v Arsenal, Chelsea v Everton and Manchester City v Bristol City

programme.title: Children in Need: 40 Fabulous Years
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan takes viewers on a funny, poignant and revealing tour across four decades of Children in Need

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a stone house in Abercynon, South Wales, a two-bed semi in West Bromwich, and a first-floor maisonette in Neasden, north-west London

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty returns with the programme exposing cases of fraud. Insurers combine forces to catch a serial fraudster making fake claims off the back of high-profile tragedies, while digital data reveals that a claim for £35,000 worth of stolen jewellery is a complete lie

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A Scottish cyclist on a round the world challenge is hit by a driver in Texas, and a family dog saves the day when a woman slips and falls on the stairs. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Danny Sebastian help two teams look for items in Shrewsbury, while Natasha Raskin Sharp explores an incredible museum and meets the collector behind it

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid has a difficult conversation with Deborah, while Zara's obsession with Harper gets out of hand. Valerie struggles to help a terminally ill woman reconnect with her estranged daughter

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: The return of the programme featuring the stories of people who bought properties they were introduced to when they appeared on Escape to the Country. Sonali Shah visits a couple who bought one of the houses presented to them on a 2019 property hunt in Bedfordshire, then meets a couple who have put down roots together in the Herefordshire countryside after renovating a property from the 1700s

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, then she and two experts transform a dressing table, a vintage suitcase and a petite sofa

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which experts travel around the UK in search of bargains before heading to an auction to find out whose haul proves most popular with buyers. In the first edition, Charles Hanson and James Braxton setting out in a 1959 MG to look for items in Oxfordshire

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present a special week of shows following the Rickshaw Challenge for Children in Need, which taking place in the grounds of the Goodwood Estate. Matt Baker provides live updates while riding alongside Team Rickshaw, and celebrity guests will be in the studio to help cheer them on

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Featuring the case of a builder with a string of unhappy customers, Plus, Steffan Powell hears from a victim of a romance fraud and the police officers who investigated the case

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick is shocked to come face to face with his ex-care worker Katy Lewis and immediately demands to know why she never told him that Frankie was his daughter - but he is stunned at her response. Lola opens up to Honey about her relationship troubles with Jay

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: As the NHS in Wales deals with one of its biggest ever challenges, many patients are left wondering what it means for them long term. In this BBC Wales Investigates programme, Wyre Davies follows those dealing with life-and-death situations during a global pandemic, speaks to doctors on the frontline and asks if more could have been done to protect vital non-Covid-19 services

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The barrister helps the second and third generations of British Jewish families affected by the Holocaust to retrace their relatives' footsteps and discover the full truth about what happened to them. Robert begins by meeting two British Jewish families who want to investigate what happened to their relatives, as well as exploring his own family's stories on both his mother's and his father's sides

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin finds out how Liverpool coped with restrictions due to Covid-19 infection rates, hearing from residents worried about the second wave, business owners pushed to the brink of despair, and those who say that living under tier three rules just isn't possible

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following life at Newcastle hair salon Longlox. Sammyjo makes a shock announcement, while there are potential promotions for the juniors and a mishap at Ashleigh's housewarming party

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The staff get used to two new colleagues at the salon, while Sammyjo surprises herself with a new love interest and has a decision to make about the junior promotions

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Roisin Conaty and Armando Iannucci joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before welcoming another group of Hollywood A-listers and British TV big shots to chat about what they've been up to recently. This week he's joined by pop queen Kylie Minogue, who chats and performs her current single Magic, British boxing champion-turned-Strictly contestant Nicola Adams, and actors Josh O'Connor and Emma Corrin, who are playing the roles of Charles and Diana in the new season of The Crown

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants

programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester City v Liverpool at Etihad Stadium and Leicester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Molineux. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present a special week of shows following the Rickshaw Challenge for Children in Need, which taking place in the grounds of the Goodwood Estate. Matt Baker provides live updates while riding alongside Team Rickshaw, and celebrity guests will be in the studio to help cheer them on

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin finds out how Liverpool coped with restrictions due to Covid-19 infection rates, hearing from residents worried about the second wave, business owners pushed to the brink of despair, and those who say that living under tier three rules just isn't possible

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick is shocked to come face to face with his ex-care worker Katy Lewis and immediately demands to know why she never told him that Frankie was his daughter - but he is stunned at her response. Lola opens up to Honey about her relationship troubles with Jay

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: Architect Michael Angus, interior designer Anna Campbell-Jones and lifestyle blogger Kate Spiers visit properties in the Borders and southern Scotland, viewing to a cliff-top home, a renovated former school, and a converted six-storey grain mill

programme.title: This Is Our Story: Inside Hearts
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Edinburgh football club during the 2019/20 season, with a losing streak at the start of leading to the sacking of manager Craig Levein. With Hearts sitting at the bottom of the table the pressure is on for chairman Ann Budge to find a new boss quickly, but she's determined to take her time and get the right person

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The barrister helps the second and third generations of British Jewish families affected by the Holocaust to retrace their relatives' footsteps and discover the full truth about what happened to them. Robert begins by meeting two British Jewish families who want to investigate what happened to their relatives, as well as exploring his own family's stories on both his mother's and his father's sides

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following life at Newcastle hair salon Longlox. Sammyjo makes a shock announcement, while there are potential promotions for the juniors and a mishap at Ashleigh's housewarming party

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The staff get used to two new colleagues at the salon, while Sammyjo surprises herself with a new love interest and has a decision to make about the junior promotions

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news with Amy Irons

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Roisin Conaty and Armando Iannucci joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before welcoming another group of Hollywood A-listers and British TV big shots to chat about what they've been up to recently. This week he's joined by pop queen Kylie Minogue, who chats and performs her current single Magic, British boxing champion-turned-Strictly contestant Nicola Adams, and actors Josh O'Connor and Emma Corrin, who are playing the roles of Charles and Diana in the new season of The Crown

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v Liverpool and Everton v Manchester United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blues visit Lincolnshire Antiques & Home Show, assisted by Nick Hall and Gary Pe. Presenter Charlie Ross gives his top tips for bagging a bargain at an antiques fair

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a new home in South Yorkshire with room enough for their children and any future grandchildren. Plus, a dog-sledding race through the woods

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: In tonight's live final just four bands remain, and they'll perform for Little Mix and the viewers at home, who will be able to vote for the winner. After an exciting journey, which band will come out on top and win the prize of a lifetime supporting Little Mix on their next UK tour?

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 11 couples return to the Strictly Ballroom for movie week, performing routines inspired by, or paying tribute to, Hollywood films. Not only do the Strictly stars have to impress the judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but they also have to impress the viewers at home

programme.title: Festival of Remembrance
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents the Royal British Legion's annual Festival of Remembrance, a programme that has been pre-recorded due to the Covid-19 pandemic, paying tribute to the those who lost their lives as victims of conflict, from the First World War to the present day

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge. Both league meetings between Everton and United ended 1-1 last term and another closely fought encounter was anticipated. The Blades came back from 2-0 down at Chelsea last term to rescue a point, but had struggled for form so far this term

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers

programme.title: Bobby (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge. The Blades came back from 2-0 down at Chelsea last term to rescue a point, but had struggled for form so far this term

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: David Dimbleby presents live coverage of the ceremony in Whitehall commemorating those who died in war, which is this year a closed event due to the coronavirus pandemic. The two-minute silence at 11am will be followed by wreath laying at the foot of the Cenotaph as the nation remembers all those who died serving their country

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Lewes high street in East Sussex, as experts Philip Serrell and Nick Hall assist two teams each afforded an hour to buy three items in the hope that they will make the greater profit at auction. Anita also finds time to meet a local historian to discover the hidden past of nearby Worthing, before the winners are determined in the saleroom

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones marks Remembrance Sunday by visiting the Brecon Beacons, where the armed forces have trained since the Second World War. James Lusted meets an army veteran who finds peace in faith and music

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents today's FA Cup first-round results, along with reports and reaction from the afternoon's big games

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to design duo Josh and Oli to repurpose an old mower, while upholsterer Leigh-Anne Treadwell goes out on a limb with her redesign of a pair of teak chairs. Sarah's challenge is to find new homes for three Persian rugs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Rich brothers David and Harry together with Arit Anderson travel to the West Midlands to help Richard and Darren create their very own slice of New York's 'High Line' garden in Solihull. The New York garden has famously turned an old disused, overground train line into a thriving garden that mixes contemporary paving, grass planting and historical, leftover train tracks. The couple want this garden to remind them of their honeymoon in the Big Apple

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith visit Cornwall, heading to Falmouth for the start of the oyster season, where successful hatching is down to the moon. They also discover how old traditions are being used in modern ways, from boatbuilding to using horses to manage an ancient landscape, while Charlotte helps out in the exotic gardens of Trelissick. Adam Henson finds out why British turkey farmers could be in for a tough time this Christmas.

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide who will be the first pair to leave the contest. With music by the Kanneh-Masons, and the Strictly professionals pay a Remembrance Day tribute to Vera Lynn

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Following Lord Asriel's tear through the worlds, Lyra and Pan journey to the strangely deserted city of Cittagazze, where they encounter Will - and reluctantly team up with the daemonless boy. Mrs Coulter interrogates a suspected heretic, while at the witch council, Lee Scoresby reveals he is seeking out an explorer who could offer Lyra protection. The return of the adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Amir Wilson and Ruth Wilson

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: As Dawn Ellson's premiership falters, Peter makes a move for the top spot - but with his enemies circling, he is forced to make a final roll of the dice. Thriller by David Hare, starring Hugh Laurie and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester City v Liverpool at Etihad Stadium and Leicester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Molineux. The Citizens overcame Liverpool 4-0 last term, but the Reds had already claimed the league title Pep Guardiola's men had won in the previous two seasons. Both meetings between the Foxes and Wolves finished goalless last season, but both sides will have been hopeful of breaking the deadlock on this occasion having made steady starts to their respective campaigns

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester United v Arsenal, Chelsea v Everton and Manchester City v Bristol City

programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a stone house in Abercynon, South Wales, a two-bed semi in West Bromwich, and a first-floor maisonette in Neasden, north-west London

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty returns with the programme exposing cases of fraud. Insurers combine forces to catch a serial fraudster making fake claims off the back of high-profile tragedies, while digital data reveals that a claim for £35,000 worth of stolen jewellery is a complete lie

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A Scottish cyclist on a round the world challenge is hit by a driver in Texas, and a family dog saves the day when a woman slips and falls on the stairs. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Scientists have discovered water on the sunlit surface of the moon for the first time but does it matter? The moon is back in vogue in terms of space exploration - the US says it will put astronauts back on the lunar surface by 2024. It's supposed to be the precursor to a manned mission to Mars. Stephen Sackur speaks to Jacob Bleacher, Chief Exploration Scientist at NASA. In this time of pandemic and climate change here on earth is space exploration a potential lifeline or a massive vanity project?

programme.desc: The story battle between Papa Mùman and Mùmantrobha get out of hand, causing some mayhem

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Matilda has the power to stop time every time she burps. First she has fun with Charlie pulling small pranks on their friends and others, who are 'frozen' in time from her power

programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to create a fake boil and make a spoon stick to the end of a nose

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis presents a music series for our times, offering musicians an opportunity to perform directly from their homes to viewing fans

programme.title: Gaol is Call (Labour of Love)
programme.desc: Drawing on her own experience, journalist Mairi Rodgers meets others to hear their stories of coping with miscarriage or stillbirth

programme.title: FUNC
programme.desc: Comedy sketch show. From President Trump calling his cousin in Lewis, to radio shows with a rude twist to the Zebras trying to board Noah's Ark, FUNC takes a comic swipe at the Gaelic and non-Gaelic world

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's helping out at an understaffed veterinary clinic. In fact, he's at the supermarket, where Fizz is trying to sell his cleaning products.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: In order to make himself an object of worship, Fizz makes people believe he's an alien coming to share his wisdom.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that the whole neighbourhood has been flooded, when in fact he is just at the local swimming pool.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Scott, Alan and Taylor put International Rescue's impressive fleet to use in helping a transport ship full of would-be colonists, who are planning to set up home on Mars. Adventure series, with the voice of Rich Hall

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Tom, forced into driving a race car created by evil Dr. Otto, terrorizes the village mice until Jerry Van Mouseling comes to their rescue

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series based on and serves as a prequel to the film. Pedro is chosen as the official hotel mummy, but he quickly loses his wraps.

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guest Ross Kemp, as well as chefs Ruth Hansom and Tom Hunt. They will be demonstrating recipes for chicken lollipops, sea bass, kippers and coq au vin, and James will also be heading off on a French adventure to Normandy

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chef Jeremy Pang and Strictly Come Dancing's Anton Du Beke to rustle up some warming winter dishes, including a hearty beef and red pea stew with thyme spinner dumplings, warm mulled cider, sweet and spicy nuts, and tropical rum crumble with nutmeg custard. Anton serves up rum and raisin bread and butter pudding, while Jeremy Pang shares his lamb rendang recipe

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and his companion Tom Kitchin head to Edinburgh, but first they take a detour to a whisky distillery in Pitlochry, where James knocks up whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once they reach the capital, Tom shares his knowledge of the local fish, James gets fitted for a kilt and learns the history of the iconic garment, and the pair enjoy a bite to eat at Tom's Michelin-starred restaurant

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a star-studded edition of the game show, with contestants Scarlett Moffatt, Jacqueline Jossa, John Barnes, David Seaman, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph racing down the aisles to win a £10,000 jackpot for the charity of their choice

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the game show, with Amanda Abbington, Hugh Dennis and Nadia Sawalha all trying to guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a 50,000 pound jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mark Foster, Charlene White, Ade Adepitan and Kevin Eldon pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Originally due to take place earlier in the year, but put on hold because of the pandemic, Emma Willis hosts the semi-final of the spinning-chair singing contest. Celebrity coaches Will.i.am, Meghan Trainor, Olly Murs and Tom Jones all hoped their act would claim victory. Now all eyes will be on the nine remaining acts to see who goes through to the series nine final on November 14

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Strictly Come Dancing stars Motsi Mabuse and Craig Revel Horwood, and comedians Rob Beckett and Sindhu Vee. Jonathan will also be chatting over video link with Alicia Keys, who will be performing from the USA

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: Host Holly Willoughby keeps captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh in check, with guests including GB hockey Olympic gold medallist Sam Quek, sports presenter Jake Humphrey and comedian Rob Beckett. Also providing the laughs are comedians Romesh Ranganathan and Seann Walsh

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. In the eight episode, the Wright-Strong family from Lancashire and the Spiliopoulou family from Hertfordshire battle for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, on a construction site, thinks he's been hired for a medieval theatre show. Fizz and Weasel bump into Magoo at the top of the tower.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is babysitting the Neighbours' toddler. The baby flees and kidnaps Fizz mistaking him for a plush toy.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is on a beach and that sailors are emptying their oil tanker right into the ocean.

programme.desc: Mr Bean chases Scrapper around Mrs Wicket's flat but her sideboard tumbles over, and when Bean sees a bit of fur poking out from under it, he thinks he has squashed Scrapper

programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp are joined by Piers Morgan, Noel Fitzpatrick, James Blunt and Perri Kiely and Jordan Banjo

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: At 694 metres in height, Pen-y-ghent is the smallest fell on the Yorkshire Three Peaks Challenge, but the dramatic summit affords far-reaching views above the Dales, with plenty of surprises en route. The peak lies 3km east of Horton in Ribblesdale and the Pennine Way links the summit to the village. As Julia reveals, the route is around 5km in length, as the Way curves initially to the north before turning east to reach the summit

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh welcomes Gyles Brandreth and Brian Conley to his Hampshire farm this week, and gives a tour of his own garden. He also meets a bird of prey native to Britain, and florist Freddie Garland shows how foraged flowers can make a wonderful display. Plus, carpenter Vikkie Lee is back with a new upcycling build, and Alan tastes the best of British coffee

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: On Remembrance Sunday, Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford visit the Imperial War Museum, Duxford. Plus, the lost diaries of First World War survivor Arthur Roberts, one of Scotland's first black soldiers

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chef Jeremy Pang and Strictly Come Dancing's Anton Du Beke to rustle up some warming winter dishes, including a hearty beef and red pea stew with thyme spinner dumplings, warm mulled cider, sweet and spicy nuts, and tropical rum crumble with nutmeg custard. Anton serves up rum and raisin bread and butter pudding, while Jeremy Pang shares his lamb rendang recipe

programme.title: Nanny McPhee and the Big Bang (2010)
programme.desc: A woman is left running the family farm alone while her husband is fighting in the Second World War. The wart-faced magical nanny is on hand to help her cope with her unruly children, their even more mischievous evacuated cousins, and her scheming brother-in-law, who wants the farm for himself. Fantasy comedy, with Emma Thompson, Maggie Gyllenhaal and Sam Kelly

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. This time around, a delivery drone drops a painful parcel, a quad-bike rolls into a lake, and a baby steals a smartphone and spends a fortune on concert tickets

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Christine, Ayesha, Michael and Nick pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Phillip Schofield, in which best friends Hirra and Safia take on Chris and his mother-in-law Lisa to try and win more than £50,000

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Comedian Ellie Taylor, Olympic boxing champion Anthony Ogogo and former Royal correspondent Jennie Bond put their wits to the test in the hope of winning £20,000 for charity. Ben Shephard hosts

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity - a Jungle Story
programme.desc: Ahead of the latest Wales-based series of I'm A Celebrity... Get Me Out of Here!, Ant and Dec take a trip down memory lane to re-live some of their favourite moments from 19 series of the hit show. Former campmates also talk about their time in the jungle, and the tables are finally turned on the hosts as they take on three terrifying Bushtucker Trials. However, it remains to be seen whether they will manage to go through with them

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Strictly Come Dancing stars Motsi Mabuse and Craig Revel Horwood, and comedians Rob Beckett and Sindhu Vee. Jonathan will also be chatting over video link with Alicia Keys, who will be performing from the USA

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. In the final episode of the series, Passenger Experience Manager Harry rushes to a gate that has had its door smashed. Meanwhile, a strange smell is discovered in cargo, and border force officers have to get to the bottom of it

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Originally due to take place earlier in the year, but put on hold because of the pandemic, Emma Willis hosts the semi-final of the spinning-chair singing contest. Celebrity coaches Will.i.am, Meghan Trainor, Olly Murs and Tom Jones all hoped their act would claim victory. Now all eyes will be on the nine remaining acts to see who goes through to the series nine final on November 14

programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Stoke-on-Trent, where Debbie Serpell has her eyes on some Meissen and Stewart Hofgartner shows interest in novelty nutcrackers, and there is more gold for Tim Hogarth

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as June, Theresa, Churchill and Winston pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bear is exasperated with Paddy, Lydia is shocked at Nate's admission, and Vinny is uneasy with Mandy's idea

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne leans on Steve during their first day in court, Paul is furious that Todd is working at Underworld, and Sean and Michael embark on a new adventure

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott rustles up some warming winter dishes, including beef and red pea stew with thyme spinner dumplings, before fellow chef Jeremy Pang shares his lamb rendang recipe. The host rounds off with something delicious and sweet - his delicious tropical fruit crumble and nutmeg custard

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve admits to Tracy that Leanne made a pass at him, Sarah takes the factory staff out for a meal, and Billy's bishop offers him a promotion

programme.title: The Diana Interview: Revenge of a Princess
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining the impact of the Princess of Wales' Panorama interview with Martin Bashir in 1995, when she revealed a royal marriage in crisis with infidelities on both sides. Friends of the princess describe how the public image of the couple's marital happiness was a mirage from the start and how a decade of betrayal was to inspire in her an anger and desire for public recognition of her private pain

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a ‘pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: Snowdon gradually falls silent in the winter months and the residents around the highest peak in England and Wales have it almost to themselves. Railwayman Steff shuts down the summit centre at Hafod Eryri as the season ends, while Alwena also calls temporary time on her Halfwacafe. Plus, wild swimmer Viv goes for a bracing winter plunge and Jo the mountain farmer finally sets foot on the summit of Snowdon for the first time.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's helping out at an understaffed veterinary clinic. In fact, he's at the supermarket, where Fizz is trying to sell his cleaning products.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: In order to make himself an object of worship, Fizz makes people believe he's an alien coming to share his wisdom.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that the whole neighbourhood has been flooded, when in fact he is just at the local swimming pool.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Scott, Alan and Taylor put International Rescue's impressive fleet to use in helping a transport ship full of would-be colonists, who are planning to set up home on Mars. Adventure series, with the voice of Rich Hall

programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Tom, forced into driving a race car created by evil Dr. Otto, terrorizes the village mice until Jerry Van Mouseling comes to their rescue

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series based on and serves as a prequel to the film. Pedro is chosen as the official hotel mummy, but he quickly loses his wraps.

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guest Ross Kemp, as well as chefs Ruth Hansom and Tom Hunt. They will be demonstrating recipes for chicken lollipops, sea bass, kippers and coq au vin, and James will also be heading off on a French adventure to Normandy

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chef Jeremy Pang and Strictly Come Dancing's Anton Du Beke to rustle up some warming winter dishes, including a hearty beef and red pea stew with thyme spinner dumplings, warm mulled cider, sweet and spicy nuts, and tropical rum crumble with nutmeg custard. Anton serves up rum and raisin bread and butter pudding, while Jeremy Pang shares his lamb rendang recipe

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and his companion Tom Kitchin head to Edinburgh, but first they take a detour to a whisky distillery in Pitlochry, where James knocks up whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once they reach the capital, Tom shares his knowledge of the local fish, James gets fitted for a kilt and learns the history of the iconic garment, and the pair enjoy a bite to eat at Tom's Michelin-starred restaurant

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a star-studded edition of the game show, with contestants Scarlett Moffatt, Jacqueline Jossa, John Barnes, David Seaman, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph racing down the aisles to win a £10,000 jackpot for the charity of their choice

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the game show, with Amanda Abbington, Hugh Dennis and Nadia Sawalha all trying to guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a 50,000 pound jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.desc: Host Holly Willoughby keeps captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh in check, with guests including GB hockey Olympic gold medallist Sam Quek, sports presenter Jake Humphrey and comedian Rob Beckett. Also providing the laughs are comedians Romesh Ranganathan and Seann Walsh

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The chalk-and-cheese detective duo are called to a private clinic where a wealthy patient has been killed. However, their investigation is made all the more difficult by the complex relationships of the victim's former fellow residents. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia take the lead roles in the second of two mystery dramas, with an all-star supporting cast including Caroline Catz, Hugh Dennis, Michele Dotrice, Freddie Fox, Suzanne Packer and Joanna Scanlan

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: In Hampshire, Becky spends her days as governess to Sir Pitt Crawley's neglected daughters, but her ambition is to get into her employer's good books and soon she is promoted to the position of his secretary. When Sir Pitt's sister Matilda pays a visit to Queen's Crawley, Becky forms a new plan and quickly charms her way to becoming her favourite new companion. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel, starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate when the body of a woman is pulled from the river during the local Oak Apple carnival. Closer inspection suggests the drowning was deliberate - and, as the detectives find out more about the villagers, they learn the killing could be linked to the death of the victim's daughter eight years previously. John Nettles and Jason Hughes play the intrepid policemen, with guest star Simon Callow

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the death of a local aristocrat who seemingly died of a coronary - shortly after declaring he had an important announcement to make. After the peer's will disappears and his lawyer is killed in an arson attack, it is clear sinister forces are at work. However, the prime suspects all appear to have cast-iron alibis. Guest starring Joss Ackland and Sian Phillips, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present a special edition of the series, dedicated to the worldwide search for the remains of soldiers killed during the First World War with no known graves. The programme follows the work of an elite all-female team at the Ministry of Defence known as the 'War Detectives', whose job is to locate the final resting places of 500,000 service personnel who lost their lives during the conflict

programme.title: The Life & Times of Captain Sir Tom
programme.desc: A profile of the former British Army officer, who captured the hearts of the nation with his fundraising walk for the NHS at the height of the Covid-19 pandemic. With access to Tom's everyday life, the film explores the man behind the blazer and hears his story, including his time on the frontline during the Burma campaign in the Second World War. Tom also recalls his teenage years, previous jobs and nursing his wife through illness - as well as a near-fatal accident he suffered just 18 months ago

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: A revelation about Charles' past spells disaster for John and jeopardises his entire future. He must act fast to keep the explosive information hidden from those who could use it to ruin his prospects. Anne receives a visit from a former employee, giving Ellis an idea of how to make some money - but she is not as cunning as she thinks. Oliver visits the mill in his search for ammunition to use against Charles, and Lady Templemore stands firm on her plans for Maria's upcoming engagement, meaning the young woman must take matters into her own hands

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: The conclusion of Julian Fellowes' drama about secrets and lies in uptight 19th-century London society. James travels to Manchester to investigate the allegations made against Charles, but soon realises there are greater threats at play. Lady Templemore employs Reggie to make Lady Maria change her mind, but her daughter will not give in so easily. Susan is in danger of losing everything after her secret is revealed, but as ever she uses her cunning to twist the situation to her own and others' benefit, while Charles is puzzled to receive a mysterious olive branch. Keen to end the feuding, he seeks out the offer of reconciliation - unaware he is being lured into danger

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: A human heart is discovered on the shores of the Loch, and forensic psychologist Blake Albrighton is brought in to join the investigating team. He quickly forms a disturbing theory about the motive behind the seemingly inexplicable murders, but Chief Inspector Frank Smilie is quick to dismiss his ideas. However, if the new arrival is correct, the once peaceful Highland community could be set to endure far more deaths, as a serial killer prowls the area. Crime drama, starring Laura Fraser, John Sessions and Don Gilet

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The son of a high-profile Jewish businessman is attacked in the grounds of a university, leaving Foyle to consider the possibility that the attack was racially motivated. Meanwhile, in London, tensions mount as charismatic right-wing leader Guy Spencer is released from prison. But is he being set up to take the spotlight off a dangerous espionage operation involving MI6? Elsewhere, Sam becomes acutely aware of the impact of the war on Adam's constituents as she encounters a young boy suffering from whooping cough. Michael Kitchen stars in the 1940s-set crime drama, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Daniel Weyman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: When recently widowed Vivien announces her love for a new man, Mark, it throws her family of adult children into chaos. The siblings' suspicions about Mark are fuelled by rising tensions in their own lives. Helen's focus on her job has led to a rift in her marriage, Jake's gambling debts have caused his wife and kids to leave him, and Natalie is lying to everybody about her secret relationship. Meanwhile, Vivien's neighbour, Mary, is simply trying to protect the family she has known for years. But in a few weeks one of these people will be lying on the beach, terribly injured - perhaps dead. Drama, starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Carry On Dick (1974)
programme.desc: Two officers of the law dedicated to catching a cheeky highwayman enlist the help of a vicar, unaware the pious pastor and his buxom housemaid are not as innocent as they seem to be. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jack Douglas, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Emma confesses to Alina that she accepted a share of the bistro robbery money and gave it to Steve to help Oliver. Abi is suspicious when Debbie makes out she is a high-flyer and is due in town for a work meeting. Michael is shocked to see Grace at the hospital, flanked by two prison officers

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Carry On Again Doctor (1969)
programme.desc: A disgraced surgeon sets up a clinic in the hope of promoting a supposedly miraculous slimming potion made from secret ingredients discovered on a remote tropical island. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jim Dale, Barbara Windsor, Charles Hawtrey, Hattie Jacques, Pat Coombs and Joan Sims

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The chalk-and-cheese detective duo are called to a private clinic where a wealthy patient has been killed. However, their investigation is made all the more difficult by the complex relationships of the victim's former fellow residents. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia take the lead roles in the second of two mystery dramas, with an all-star supporting cast including Caroline Catz, Hugh Dennis, Michele Dotrice, Freddie Fox, Suzanne Packer and Joanna Scanlan

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: In Hampshire, Becky spends her days as governess to Sir Pitt Crawley's neglected daughters, but her ambition is to get into her employer's good books and soon she is promoted to the position of his secretary. When Sir Pitt's sister Matilda pays a visit to Queen's Crawley, Becky forms a new plan and quickly charms her way to becoming her favourite new companion. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel, starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A misunderstanding between Sid and Betty leads to chaos and confusion. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
