programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George travels to the Cornish village of Charlestown, where Charlotte and Joey have bought a public loo that they are planning to turn into a luxury clifftop holiday home - at a cost of £65,000. He also meets a Bristol family who are making their own safari tent, completes his tour of New Zealand with a visit to an underground house, and he and Will Hardie finally unveil their prototype futuristic home

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Jan Fursier has discovered in one of her father Reg's diaries an illustrated guide to building a 1930s caravan, drawn up while he was being held in a Japanese prisoner of war camp. George Clarke and Will Hardie attempt to bring the caravan to life, leading to an emotional reunion in the same Welsh national park where Reg took the family on holidays once the Second World War was over

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect recalls projects from Amazing Spaces, revisiting his favourite caravan builds, wilderness lodges and garden retreats. In this instalment, he considers the caravan-inspired treehouse in the Highlands of Scotland, a mirror-clad cube in Sweden and a UFO construction nestled amongst the branches. Plus, a trip to Tuscany and George's own star gazing treehouse in Kielder National Park, Northumberland

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sex and the City fan Jose Stewart hosts the first dinner party in Lincolnshire for teacher Skip Schultz, tattooist Indy Nijjar, entrepreneur Sharon Wright and admin worker Jessica Carroll, but struggles to concentrate on her Spanish-themed menu, opting instead to spend her time on Twitter. A game of truth or dare then brings out some interesting revelations, as well as an unlikely romantic encounter

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: History teacher Skip Schultz hosts the second party in Lincolnshire, planning an evening to celebrate his American roots. While the guests are impressed with the starter, the main course proves a confusing flop, and Skip has to rely on his entertainment to save the day

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Sharon Wright hosts Lincolnshire's third party, hoping to impress with dishes of seared scallops, crispy-top pork belly, and chocolate praline tart, which she has repeatedly practised in preparation for the evening. The businesswoman has a strict schedule and has considered her guests' every need, even hiring a gardener to ensure the yard is in full bloom

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tattoo artist Indy Nijjar hosts a summer-themed dinner party in Lincolnshire, instructing his guests to bring swimwear with them. In a last-ditch bid to score high marks, he decides to serve his dessert in a unique place, but will have to wait and see whether it is enough to win the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Admin worker Jessica Carroll hosts the final party in Lincolnshire. Despite demonstrating a distinct lack of culinary knowledge over the course of the week, including asking teacher Skip Schultz if his pumpkin pie contained pumpkin, she hopes her Chinese meal and unusual pet will impress the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Brighton Surf hotel, where Jeremy and Elena Mann hope their hands-off hosting style will win the approval of their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Number 16, in Greenwich, London, owned by actor and antiques dealer, Robert Gray. Alas, Jan and Les find the dusty decor off-putting

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week comes from the Fleur de Lis in Sandwich, Kent, with hosts Jan and Les Fisher convinced their traditional place will impress the others

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit of the week is to Dale End House near Matlock in the Peak District, where owner Sarah Summers is dreading the arrival of Robert after their confrontation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day sees the B&B owners meet for the last time, with the Brighton Surf Hotel's Jeremy and Elena Mann wanting to defend their no-breakfast remote-hosting concept

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Claire Wier hosts the first party in Worcestershire and hopes her low-fat menu and pre-dinner exercise routines are enough to secure her the £1,000 prize. However, her guests are disappointed that the evening meal is lacking in cream, fat, oil and butter

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty pageant queen Pippa Langhorne throws the second party in Worcestershire, and hopes to impress her fellow diners with a sophisticated Midsummer Night's Dream-themed event. However, her starter fails to win the approval of shellfish-hating Claire Wier, and Tim Lawson disapproves on the basis of his friendship with fish. Everyone is surprised as the hostess brings out an unusual dessert, and a guest appearance by her singing dog Buddy rounds off a memorable evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Publican Tim Lawson hosts the third dinner party in Worcestershire, laying on a country-themed evening featuring a novel take on prawn cocktail and a creamy main course. Chris Sheppard finds himself confronted about his tan, while tension mounts between Pippa Langhorne and Claire Wier, who takes issue with her fellow diner's claims to have a white bread allergy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teresa Cox hosts the fourth dinner party challenge from Worcestershire and sets her store on wooing the guests with a lobster starter and an extravagant main course. The menu seems to go down well with the diners, but when the conversation turns to the paranormal there are mixed reactions

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Chris Sheppard hosts the final dinner party in Worcestershire, throwing a black-tie-themed evening. He impresses some of his guests with his home-made sweet chilli sauce and potato towers - although Claire Wier finds the presentation pretentious - before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Autumn at Jimmy's Farm
programme.desc: Following life on Jimmy Doherty's farm during the autumn months, as the team prepare the farm and wildlife park for the incoming seasonal changes. In the final episode, Jimmy and his team prepare for the arrival of Barbary apes, the park's first ever primates, and the farm also says goodbye to two young reindeer calves

programme.title: Emergency Rescue: Air, Land & Sea
programme.desc: In the Lake District, Langdale Ambleside Mountain Rescue Team rush to Andrew, who has a potential limb threatening injury. Newquay Search and Rescue help skipper Shaun, who has severed his finger catching mussels out at sea. And Keswick Mountain Rescue Team assist a fellow team member's wife, Trish, after a fall on Ullock Pike

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Paramedics across Britain treat patients fighting for their lives. In Nottingham, Selina Conway is dispatched to a 76-year-old man who has collapsed in his garden, while Liam Wakefield heads to the home of an 18-year-old who has stopped breathing. In Essex, Sadie Bentley and recently qualified Anna Tansley are called by the coastguard to help resuscitate a man found floating in the sea

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Aaron, 31, is rushed to St George's after overturning his car onto metal railings during a high-speed collision. He has a serious laceration to his face where a spiked railing narrowly missed his eye socket. Cameras also follow Fiona, 65, who is rushed to the hospital after falling over in a restaurant and cutting her knee during her friend Evelyn's 70th birthday party

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay checks into the luxurious Lakeview Hotel on Lake Chelan, Washington, where the bedrooms are empty and the hotel's restaurant and ice-cream parlour are failing. Gordon confronts owner Brent's inappropriate and miserly behaviour, but is it enough to repair the establishment's reputation?

programme.title: Emergency Rescue: Air, Land & Sea
programme.desc: In the Lake District, Langdale Ambleside Mountain Rescue Team rush to Andrew, who has a potential limb threatening injury. Newquay Search and Rescue help skipper Shaun, who has severed his finger catching mussels out at sea. And Keswick Mountain Rescue Team assist a fellow team member's wife, Trish, after a fall on Ullock Pike

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire bull terrier puppy called Rex receives treatment for a badly deformed elbow, and the team carry out a complicated procedure to realign the bones. Meanwhile, a cross-breed called Piggy with spinal problems leaves its family facing a difficult decision, and Noel devises an unorthodox cure for a stray cat with a badly fractured leg

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Father Jack is hauled off to the local old priests' home to be treated for a strange medical complaint. He is replaced by a younger man - who proceeds to drive everyone crazy by playing jungle music at ear-splitting volume for hours on end. Dermot Morgan and Frank Kelly star

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp meets the ladies at the Merched y Wawr - the Welsh version of the Women's Institute - and they show her how to make petals using gelatine

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John and Poppy are hoping to retire to France's Lot-et-Garonne region, so Ben Hillman sets out to find them the perfect place for their £175,000 budget

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: It's crunch time for Jonathan and Michael who are facing their investors, but their newest idea - to keep goats - does not go down well. Philip and Angelina host the big anniversary party they've been spending weeks preparing for, but with broken toilets and 60 guests to contend with, how will it go? Plus, single mother Fiona battles to get her dining room ready to show off to the mayor

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in Boroughbridge, North Yorkshire, helping teachers Nick and Fiona build a large warehouse-inspired home on the side of a canal

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to the Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. Eventually, the winner is finally revealed

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: It's nightfall at Chester and the zoo's 800 nocturnal inhabitants start to wake up. Aardvark Koos is showing a potentially romantic interest in his sister Himba, but the zoo needs to ensure that their animals are breeding from the right genetic pool. It's also time for mouse-deer Una to leave her parents and meet a mate

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge follows the fortunes of Brit Mandy and her American business partner Steve, who have decided to build a swimming pool to entice more paying guests to their stunning cliff-edge chateau in the Dordogne. Desperate to increase income at his chateau, Michael invites Angel Adoree to help out with an experiment to do afternoon tea for the locals

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets Lori and Morgan, a couple building a unique modern home on a tight budget in Elsternwick, Victoria

programme.title: Grand Designs Revisited
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Helen Gould and Phil Reddy five years after they built an earth-sheltered home in Cumbria. The presenter finds out if the house has survived without water leaking in and whether it has delivered the eco-friendly life the couple desired

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The return of the programme showcasing engineering wonders, beginning with a team building a 35-room, fully functional hotel out of snow and ice in the village of Jukkasjarvi in northern Sweden

programme.title: Secrets of the Superfactories
programme.desc: The inner workings of green car factory VW Chattanooga, nitrogen-fresh Illy coffee, classic yellow JCB backhoe loaders and robot-made Strandberg guitars

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 84-year-old Neil who has fallen down the stairs at home, and his wife Jenny reflects on the shock of realising that their life may never be the same again. Orthopaedic registrar Sadia is concerned about a serious leg fracture to 25-year-old Danny, who was knocked off his motorbike, and may have other hidden internal injuries

programme.title: Guy Martin vs the Robot Car
programme.desc: The presenter explores the world of automated self-driving vehicles, attempting to build one of his own in his shed. He takes a trip to Budapest for a ride in a completely autonomous vehicle, which is being tested on public roads for the first time. Finally, he heads to Silverstone, to take on a computer-controlled car in a race

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The return of the programme showcasing engineering wonders, beginning with a team building a 35-room, fully functional hotel out of snow and ice in the village of Jukkasjarvi in northern Sweden

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick's first patient is labradoodle Mitzi, who has been hit by a car - and Noel's biggest concern is that she may have suffered nerve damage, leaving her owners fearing the worst. Options are limited for 10-month-old German pointer Hector, who is struggling to walk - he must undergo spinal surgery or face the prospect of being put to sleep, while a Leonberger with a cancerous leg undergoes an operation to remove the tumour

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Scotland to find out about a mysterious condition threatening salmon stocks, Kate Quilton heads Down Under to learn about kangaroo farming and Matt Tebbutt goes behind the scenes of the annual race to get new season asparagus onto the nation's supermarket shelves

programme.desc: The former wrestler challenges eight contestants to triumph over a series of gruelling physical endurance tests

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. They design a vehicle for elderly people and put it to the test in Dorset with the help of three pensioners. Meanwhile, James May gets acquainted with a revamped Range Rover in London before pitting the luxurious off-roader against an autonomous military machine in Nevada. Actor James McAvoy tackles the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef's final trip takes him to Morocco, where he samples the delights of couscous, tagines and mint tea in the country's bazaars, before travelling along the old silk route in south-eastern Turkey to taste kebabs, another of the Mediterranean's favourite foods

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank explore two barns and an attic that are overflowing with unique oddities, while Danielle finds she cannot resist joining them on their hunts for treasure

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank visit a funeral home and a prosthetic limb factory, and examine a collection of vintage toys

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May immerse themselves in nostalgia and try to prove that hot hatches from their younger days are better than today's equivalents. Jeremy grabs the keys to a Volkswagen Golf GTI, Richard takes the wheel of a Vauxhall Nova SRi and James May opts for the Ford Fiesta XR2i, and the trio set off on a road trip that includes a hill climb and a visit to a supermarket. However, their adventure concludes at an army training ground in Wales, where they and their steeds must face the heavily armed Top Gear Police Department. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville blasts the Reasonably Priced Car around the test track

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: The work taking place in one of Britain's leading police car workshops, as bespoke vehicles are built and repairs made to ones that have been out in the field. In the first episode, John Heussi finds himself in need of a new riot van, and stress-testing the old one helps him design a new one

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: Dave has a BMW X5 armed response vehicle build to oversee, and is also tasked with repairing a car damaged during a high-speed chase

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers are on alert for a cartel ambush as they transfer a Sicario to the Mexican military. A random inspection of a pick-up truck reveals 32kg of narcotics

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Documentary with unrestricted access to America's Customs and Border Protection working on the bridges that separate Texas and Mexico, following the work of the many officers who police the bridges and keep America safe. In the first episode, a shipment of cardboard packaging hides a deadly secret, and heroin disguised as a laptop baffles the world's most advanced narcotic detection device

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Celebrity guests Fern Britton, Richard E Grant, Sanjeev Bhaskar and Martin Clunes join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show in which contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon presents

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy's discussion about whether they should have another child descends into psychological warfare. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy think they have bought the perfect Christmas present for seven-year old Charlie and five-year-old twins Benji and Molly. However, their pride in their parenting is short-lived when the youngsters' grandparents buy exactly the same gift and beat them to the punch by handing it over on Christmas Eve. Lee ventures into the streets on the night before Christmas and faces a desperate race against time to find a suitable replacement. Lee Mack and Sally Bretton star in a festive episode of the sitcom

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the mid-engined Alfa Romeo 4C on the shores of Lake Como in Italy - but his enjoyment of the lightweight vehicle is disturbed by the arrival of Jeremy Clarkson, who demands a race against his quad bike. Jeremy also visits Belgium, putting the McLaren P1 hypercar to the test in Bruges and around the Spa-Francorchamps Formula One circuit - and its combined power output of more than 900bhp generated from electricity and turbocharged petrol V8 power is likely to cause astonishment. Meanwhile, James May spends time at Camp Bastion in Afghanistan to learn about the army's vehicle redeployment efforts, and Thor star Tom Hiddleston tries to coax the best time he can muster from the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three petrolheads demonstrate their love of small cars with a trip to Ukraine. Jeremy Clarkson chooses a Volkswagen Up for the journey, while Richard Hammond takes a Ford Fiesta and James May opts for a Dacia Sandero. On their adventure, the trio absorb the history of the Crimean Peninsula, including a visit to an old submarine pen - but the carefree exploration looks likely to become a chore when Jeremy, Richard and James are challenged to a long drive north through the expansive country, during which they must combat serious boredom. And as if that wasn't arduous enough, a new task is set when they arrive in Kiev - a trip into the wasteland of Chernobyl. This edition also features a track test of the Danish Zenvo ST1 supercar and James Blunt returns for another drive in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.desc: Big Zuu is going regal as he heads to Royal Leamington Spa to meet and eat with comedian and actor Guz Khan. Leamington was once a place Queen Victoria visited, now it's the mandems turn

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: In Houston, Eddie faces the 'grim reaper' in a crawfish challenge featuring the fearsome reaper pepper. He also devours some southern comfort food and pulls a plane down a runway

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie Hall follows in Rocky's footsteps with physical challenges at a famed Philadelphia boxing gym - and his very own hunger games in the infamous Philly Cheesesteak Challenge

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Comedian Katherine Ryan joins host Richard Ayoade on a 48-hour ram raid of the Big Apple. The duo's packed-to-overflow schedule sees them scale tall buildings, grab some culture in museums large and small and even attempt to rollerblade. Experiences include trips to the Metropolitan Museum of Art and the Rockefeller Centre, queuing at the hottest patisserie in Manhattan for a cronut, and enjoying a free ferry ride to the Statue of Liberty. There's even time for Richard to reach the point of meltdown

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Time-conscious traveller Richard Ayoade is joined by actor Paul Rudd for a brisk romp round the Finnish capital. To get their bearings the pair head straight to the ladies toilet in the Torni hotel, which offers the best view of the city. They then go kayaking and snack on some reindeer jerky at the city's Hakaniemi market. Richard and Paul also visit not one, but two saunas - one of which can be found in a well-known burger chain. The duo also visit the wilderness island of Seurasaari, go foraging, and finish up in a sweet shop

programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Rob Beckett plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by rising comedy stars Jen Brister and Darren Harriott

programme.desc: Mike and Frank find a rare comic-book and visit a house that is believed to be haunted

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike comes across a 150-year-old guitar that could be the valuable find he has been searching for

programme.title: Modern Wheels or Classic Steals
programme.desc: Rebecca Jackson and Elo help family man Paul find a new car, choosing between a modern BMW and a classic Mercedes-Benz

programme.title: Modern Wheels or Classic Steals
programme.desc: Rebecca Jackson and Elo help recently retired Bik find a new car, choosing between a practical family car or a present to himself

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson heads to Italy to get behind the wheel of the exclusive, hand-crafted Alfa Romeo Disco Volante, which is made by coachbuilder Touring Superleggera and based on the manufacturer's already rare 8C Competizione sports car. Richard Hammond asserts authority over the sand dunes of Abu Dhabi in the six-wheel-drive Mercedes-Benz G63 AMG 6x6, while James May defies his status as Captain Slow by blasting two Caterhams around the test track - the minimalist entry-level Seven 160 and the full-fat supercharged Seven 620R. Comedian Jack Whitehall sets a lap time in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond visits Abu Dhabi's Yas Marina circuit to drive the Porsche 918 Spyder, a four-wheel-drive hybrid hypercar capable of astonishing speeds thanks to the 886 horsepower it can generate from its screaming V8 petrol engine and electric motors. Jeremy Clarkson and James May join forces to create a public information film to promote safer cycling, with their efforts presented to a panel of experts including members of Westminster Council and Olympic gold medal-winning rider Chris Boardman. Meanwhile, Jeremy - who has promised to change his name to Jennifer if the Porsche is faster around the test track than the McLaren P1 driven earlier in the series - compares the seventh-generation Volkswagen Golf GTI to BMW's critically acclaimed M135i. Breaking Bad star Aaron Paul fires up the Reasonably Priced Car to see what lap time he can achieve

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef takes a gastronomic road trip to Istanbul through the countries of the former Byzantine Empire - a melting pot of East and West. His journey begins in the city of Venice, and on the idyllic Greek island of Symi he cooks some of his favourite Venetian dishes including seafood risotto, tiramisu and gnocchi with spider crab

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Russia as the motoring magazine returns, taking part in a race across the urban landscape of St Petersburg. James defends the honour of the car in a very small Renault, Richard tries to prove that pedalling is faster by riding a bicycle, while Jeremy attempts to demonstrate that water is the quickest way across town by piloting a hovercraft. To make matters more complicated, all three presenters are racing not only one another but also the Stig, who is making his way toward the finish line by public transport. Plus, the new Lamborghini Huracan is put through its paces and singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran takes to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie Hall, winner of the 2017 World's Strongest Man contest, tours the USA on a quest to conquer the biggest and best eye-watering food and physical challenges. Eddie lands in Florida Keys to attempt a conch-fritter eating record, do some serious big-game fishing and try what's billed as the best Key Lime Pie on earth

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Johnny Vegas accompanies Richard Ayoade on a brief jaunt to the United Arab Emirates and the oil-rich city of Dubai. Richard checks into a suite boasting five balconies at the Atlantis Hotel, on the world's largest man-made island. The luxury continues with a city tour by seaplane and a taste of the world's most expensive ice-cream. The pair enter the biggest shopping centre in the world, where they enjoy a camel sandwich and a camelccino, and visit the observation deck of the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Sophie Duker, Ed Gamble and Mark Simmons and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies dishes out the absurd A-list challenges once more. Aisling Bea, Bob Mortimer, Mark Watson, Nish Kumar and Sally Phillips are driven to ever more absurd lengths in the battle to lay their sticky fingers on Taskmaster Greg's golden head

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from May 2018, with comedy writer Andy Hamilton and politician Sayeeda Warsi joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Footballer-turned-property expert Dion Dublin, comedians Bob Mortimer and Lucy Porter, and the lovely Debbie McGee join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: Jon is joined by Rob Beckett, Ed Gamble and Lou Sanders to address worries concerning Health. Are we due a plague? Is noise the silent killer?

programme.title: Judge Romesh
programme.desc: The comic holds court over real-life disputes, featuring an extra who is costing his friend acting work. Plus, rappers Harvey and Romeo argue over lyrics copyrights

programme.title: Judge Romesh
programme.desc: The 'judge' is faced with mediating a dispute in his own family, while two Yorkshire wrestlers fight it out in court

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: The historian reveals a story of invasion in Britain spanning millennia, beginning with him felling a tree with a flint axe in Kent, and riding an Iron Age chariot. Sam Willis also searches for clues of invasion at Silbury Hill, and tracks down evidence of the Beaker people, who brought ceramics, metalwork and beakers to Britain

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: Sam Willis tells the story of the Barbary Corsaire pirates who made their headquarters on Lundy Island. He also sheds light on the little-known tale of King Louis the Lion, a French King who invaded Britain in the 13th century

programme.title: Sings the Beatles
programme.desc: A look back through the BBC's archives at memorable covers of classic Beatles songs. Included are a performance of Day Tripper by Sandie Shaw, Shirley Bassey's version of Something, the Carpenters' rendition of Help!, Oasis's reinvention of I Am the Walrus, and Joe Cocker's chart-topping interpretation of With a Little Help from My Friends. Plus, Paul McCartney's solo performance of Lady Madonna

programme.title: The Rolling Stones at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of the band's performances from the BBC archives, in a programme originally broadcast in 2012 to celebrate their 50th anniversary. Songs include The Last Time, Let's Spend The Night Together, Get Off of My Cloud, Honky Tonk Women, Gimme Shelter, Miss You and Start Me Up. Plus, footage of the band performing 19th Nervous Breakdown on Top of the Pops in 1966, which was used in a 1960s BBC documentary about women with depression

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels the length and breadth of the country with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook to discover how the railways have affected people and find out what remains of Bradshaw's Britain. He begins in Liverpool by learning to speak Scouse, before researching the first railway fatality and exploring the origins of the Eccles cake

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The second leg of Michael Portillo's journey from Liverpool to Scarborough takes him from Manchester to Bury. Along the way, he finds out about the life of Victorian train enthusiast George Bradshaw, gets fitted for a trilby hat in Denton, and learns how railways helped contribute to the nation's love affair with fish and chips

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey, travelling on the Embsay and Bolton Abbey steam railway, finding out about Roman discoveries in York, and taking to the air in the Network Rail helicopter

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey inspired by George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook. He searches for the last liquorice grower in Pontefract, finds out how the railways transformed Hull into one of the largest white-fish ports in the world, and goes fishing for sea bass in Bridlington

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In East Yorkshire, Michael Portillo goes bird-watching on the cliffs of Flamborough Head before travelling to Filey in North Yorkshire, where he learns to decipher traditional knitting patterns. He then continues his journey to the Victorian seaside resort of Scarborough, and views a 4,000-year-old skeleton called Gristhorpe Man

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the first leg of his second journey, Michael Portillo heads to Kirkcaldy in Fife, finding out along the way how the mill town of Preston in Lancashire gave birth to the Temperance Movement. In Blackpool, he enjoys a performance in the Tower Ballroom and discovers the railways had a direct impact on the town's birth as a resort. After a stroll along the pier with local comedian Joey Blower, Michael visits Morecambe Bay

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Martyn Colbeck and Cynthia Moss examine the legacy of Echo the elephant, who died in 2009 after being followed on film during the final chapter of her life, having guided her family for half a century. With a drought devastating the animals' home in the shadow of Kilimanjaro, a 38-strong band of relatives and descendants must overcome the loss of their leader, hunger and the threat of poachers to survive. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: Extended programme exploring the world's only sloth orphanage in Costa Rica, and meeting the characters that live there, including Alfie, who suffers from stunted growth

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the different methods animals use to attract a mate, revealing how inventive, loving and complex they can be. A female polar bear tries to revive a male's interest in her by turning cartwheels in front of him, while a bird of paradise tidies obsessively as he prepares a stage for his dance. While other lemurs are fighting for the right to breed, one sidles up to a female and wins favour by wafting his scent at her, and silverback gorilla seduction involves staring with a fixed grin

programme.desc: Part two of two. Mike Brewer and Edd China continue their project to get a 1.8-litre VW Mark 1 Golf GTi in prime condition to go back out on the road

programme.title: Chasing Classic Cars
programme.desc: Wayne flies to Maryland to inspect a collection including a Jaguar XKE, an Austin Healy 3000 and a Lincoln Continental

programme.title: Chasing Classic Cars
programme.desc: Wayne goes after a 500hp 1952 Allard J2X and Lamborghini Miura. While both owners are eager to sell, it looks like they might be getting cold feet

programme.title: Mega Shippers
programme.desc: Massive machines are brought in to move a raised dual carriageway. Plus, a team races against time to underload a shipment of timber

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: An insight into how the minerals that are an essential ingredient in glass cookware are mined, and a look at how money is kept safe and secure inside cash machines

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How the alcoholic spirit aquavit is made, and how kangaroos help protect motorcycle riders in a crash. Plus, a look at the cleaning of New York's St Patrick's Cathedral

programme.title: Plane Reclaimers
programme.desc: The team is caught off-guard when a Boeing 737 flies in early from Amsterdam. Once the lads get going, an apprentice is put under pressure when he works on the expensive engines

programme.title: Combat Dealers
programme.desc: Bruce Crompton heads to Dunkirk after finding a picture in his attic that was given to him by a soldier who was there during the famous evacuation

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard delves into the history of print on London's Fleet Street. Plus, in Devon, he indulges his passion for architectural salvage

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Kurt gets lost while hauling a hut across Australia, while Johnny drags a stack of steel - and a dust cloud - along an intimidating desert track

programme.title: MotoGP Highlights
programme.desc: Highlights of all the action from the MotoGP 2020 motorcycle racing World Championship in Spain

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Skilled 'pickers' Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz rummage through a collection of Ford and automobile industry items. Plus, Danielle examines the patent model of an unusual airplane

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Modern-day treasure hunter Drew Pritchard makes a startling find at a 13th-century house in the Lake District. Later, he visits a classic car collector in Hertfordshire

programme.title: Aussie Gold Hunters
programme.desc: As the season closes, the Gold Gypsies are plagued by more bad luck and the Victoria Diggers face a death in the family. The Poseidon Crew hopes to continue its run of big gold

programme.title: Gold Divers
programme.desc: Shawn pushes himself, and his equipment, to the limit as he seeks new gold. Meanwhile, at Sledge Island, Zeke suffers a dangerous reaction to working in deeper water

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: The rounding up of escaped pythons in Florida, the making of Panama hats, and how drivers are kept safe in the world's longest tunnel

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: The construction of working miniature airports, the design of houses to withstand hurricanes and the cultivation of rhubarb in the dark

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer and Ant Anstead enter the market for iconic American SUVs with a 1988 Jeep Grand Wagoneer. However, the vehicle has sagging suspension and defective four-wheel drive

programme.title: Combat Dealers
programme.desc: Bruce Crompton heads to Dunkirk after finding a picture in his attic that was given to him by a soldier who was there during the famous evacuation

programme.title: American Chopper
programme.desc: Orange County Choppers plays host to the RJR memorial car show, and Paul Teutul Jr enlists the aid of the fire department to organise a pancake breakfast

programme.title: Mega Shippers
programme.desc: A heavy lift vessel takes onboard a 23,000-tonne oil rig. Massive machines are brought in to move a raised dual carriageway and a team races against time to underload a shipment of timber. Plus, a blanket of fog keeps one of the world's biggest airplanes grounded

programme.title: Mighty Trains
programme.desc: A look at the dizzy heights of the Glacier Express, which uses updated versions of old technology to ascend the steepest slopes on its route across the Swiss Alps

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The manufacture of bowling balls, barber poles, felt and radar guns

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The manufacture of copper pipe fittings, cylinder music boxes, pepper mills and hot-rod steering columns

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Van driver Annie Wroe serves up a racy-themed menu for the opening dinner party in Plymouth, with the starter named Something Fishy Going On, a main course called Saucy Pork Loins with Veg and a dessert of Chocolicious with a Twist. However, former Navy officer Charlie Brunel refuses to eat the main course and all the guests are alarmed by the after-dinner entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Car service manager Suzanne Hyde hosts the second party in Plymouth, and after the heated exchanges of the previous dinner, prepares for another tense evening. She hopes to win over her fellow diners with her simple style of cooking, but the food is overshadowed when she argues with previous hostess Annie Wroe

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third day of the competition in Plymouth. Former sailor Tony Wade thinks of himself as the best cook in the group, and prepares a range of complicated dishes to prove it. He also hopes to ease relations between Annie Wroe and Suzanne Hyde by sitting them next to each other, which is a risky undertaking

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment manager Sarah Joy hosts the penultimate dinner party in Plymouth. She hopes to create a happier atmosphere after a series of disagreements, but outspoken guest Suzanne has other ideas. Later, an after-dinner disco gives Charlie the opportunity to perform a Scottish jig

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former Navy soldier Charlie Brunel hosts the final dinner party in Plymouth, and plans to get his guests into the party mood by organising a pirate-themed evening, complete with fancy dress and a fish dish, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Choir teacher Dionne hosts the first of five parties from in and around Leeds, hoping her menu inspired by family favourites will impress Billy, Jane, Darren and Denver

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Finance director Jane hosts the second night in Leeds. Her plan to grab the grand is to treat her guests to a night of fine dining, but she has her work cut out. Drama teacher Denver is already on the back foot with Jane, after taking offence to being called a 'goth' on the first night. Car painter Darren is concerned that Jane's menu of home-made ravioli, pork and panna cotta could win the week, but he's less impressed when it gets to the table. Painter and decorator Billy has noticed a chilling change among some of the other diners and Dionne is just glad her night is out the way so she can enjoy the rest of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Drama teacher and DJ Denver hosts the third dinner party in Leeds, with a Spanish-inspired menu that also includes a prosecco van and stilt walkers on arrival

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Billy serves up a menu of dinner-party favourites on the fourth evening in Leeds, and after an appearance from a Frank Sinatra impersonator, he has high hopes of pocketing the cash

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final night of the contest in Leeds finds Darren planning to put a fancy twist on what seems to be a basic menu. Denver still hasn't forgiven Jane to likening her to a goth on the first evening and the fact that Darren is serving goat's cheese rubs salt in her wounds. Billy is concerned about the amount of mustard Darren is using in his dishes and mother-of-five Dionne causes a commotion when she offers up a hug to Jane

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Tracey and Stephen from Staffordshire search for a new home in the Spanish province of Malaga with a budget of £100,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies draws on her property expertise to help find Neil and Linda from Lanarkshire a rustic holiday home around the picturesque town of Antequera in southern Spain

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding host a 1980s-themed episode of the contest. The signature challenge features a classic quiche, while the technical round requires the bakers to master a popular retro sweet treat. For their final showstopper task, the contestants must keep their cool while preparing an ice cream cake before judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood pick this week's star baker and decide who will be leaving the tent

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, where he meets a stubborn owner who doesn't react well to criticism. Tom bought the restaurant for his daughter Andrea, but six years later he's still refusing to let her run it. Can the chef break Tom's iron grip on the business and persuade him to change his outdated approach?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Beaver, Pennsylvania, where he meets a brother-and-sister team who find it almost impossible to work together. Their relationship is so fractured that they're failing to save their father's Italian restaurant Levanti's from being overwhelmed by local competition, and the situation has become so desperate that their dad is in danger of losing his life savings

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sam's Mediterranean Kabob Room in Monrovia, California, and within minutes of his arrival, is caught in the crossfire of an argument between the owner, his wife and their children, all of whom are feeling the pressure of working seven days a week. Can the chef save the business and mend the warring family's frayed relationships?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the coastal community of Long Beach, California, to save an Italian restaurant that was opened in 1958 and now under the misguided leadership of Nino, who blamed by brother Michael and sister Carina for the eatery's failings. Their 78-year-old mother Inge desperate for Nino to turn the business around, but the feuding family members will have to solve their differences first

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the week's biggest and best shows

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the week's biggest and best shows

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back contributors whose differences were the making, or breaking, of their dates, including pharmacist Peter and German shoe manufacturer Gregor. Elsewhere, 26-year-old Nathan may have made a name for himself on the Watford club scene, but could he get on the right track with businesswoman Jade?

programme.title: Heston's Feasts
programme.desc: Fay Ripley, Dan Snow, Alex Norton, Hardeep Singh Kohli, Jenni Falconer and Caroline Feraday join Heston Blumenthal for the second of his extravagant dinners. Taking the Regency period as his inspiration, the chef conjures up a fairytale feast owing debts of gratitude to a selection of weird and wonderful childhood fantasies. He prepares a Cinderella-style pumpkin for starters, and a stuffed boar's head taken from Snow White, before treating his guests to an edible Hansel & Gretel house

programme.title: Alton Towers: A Rollercoaster Year
programme.desc: Documentary following the reopening of the theme park after a long period of closure due to Covid-19. Staff have only 12 days to ensure that the rides are both fully operational and comply with new health and safety guidelines, which requires some major rethinking. The programme follows both employees returning to work after months on furlough as well as families visiting the park for some much-needed escapism after being in lockdown

programme.title: Deliveroo: Secrets of Your Takeaway
programme.desc: The behind-the-scenes story of how Britain's fastest-growing food delivery company has revolutionised the takeaway industry and how they, and their restaurant partners, are navigating the storm of the coronavirus pandemic. Cameras follow the teams of computer programmers, app developers and salespeople as they manage a network of restaurants and delivery riders that together have transformed the takeaway experience

programme.title: Autumn at Jimmy's Farm
programme.desc: Following life on Jimmy Doherty's farm during the autumn months, as the team prepare the farm and wildlife park for the incoming seasonal changes. The team prepare for the arrival of some new animals which could prove a handful for Jimmy and the rangers, and wildlife in the woodland starts to harvest the summer's bounty to get ready for winter

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: DIY fanatic Fiona contends with sub-zero temperatures as she sets out to renovate a chateau bedroom for her daughter, who is due to visit in two months, while new owners Erin and JB aim to transform the first floor of their property's south wing

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: in and JB turn their attention to transforming their chateau's ruined orangery into a top-class events venue. When the concrete is delivered for a new floor, JB must race against time to find a missing piece of kit before the concrete sets. Tensions bubble to the surface as a new spa is delivered to Tim and Sasha's chateau, while Fiona celebrates her birthday and continues to battle sub-zero temperatures as she renovates a bedroom for her daughter Rochelle

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB test run the orangery as a new events space by hosting their A Midsummer Night's Dream-themed party for a group of friends visiting from the UK. In the south of France, Tim and Sasha set out to turn their chateau's original bathing pool - which has sat empty for 15 years - into a unique feature for a new wellness garden. Plus, Fiona unveils a plaque and hosts a party to celebrate her chateau's 845th birthday. To ensure the drinks are served at the right temperature, she transforms one of the chateau's old dungeons into the perfect wine cellar

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Tracey and Stephen from Staffordshire search for a new home in the Spanish province of Malaga with a budget of £100,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies draws on her property expertise to help find Neil and Linda from Lanarkshire a rustic holiday home around the picturesque town of Antequera in southern Spain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lecturer Maxine and paramedic team leader Cormac got married in the scenic northern region of the Dordogne in France, and now want Ben Hillman's help to find them their dream holiday home there. They have a budget of just over £100,000 and would ideally like a property with lots of outside space, just a short walk from the town of Riberac.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help Cambridgeshire couple Wendy and Pete, who cannot decide whether they want to move to Great Yarmouth in Norfolk or Almeria in Spain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tracey and Iain from south Wales are looking to buy a holiday home in Ibiza after they fell in love with the island on a family holiday six years ago. They're especially drawn to the pretty town of Santa Eularia and have set their hearts on somewhere with a sea view, so call upon the skills of property expert Ben Hillman to find them the home of their dreams for their £275,000 budget

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. The estranged couple's two children are struggling to save the business from their parents' disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to New Orleans to visit Zeke's, a much-loved restaurant that has seen standards slip since the original owner died. The new proprietors, husband and wife duo Darryl and Ellen Cortello, focused all the efforts on the financial side of things, so it is up to Gordon to show them that they first need to love food to make any profit

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming

programme.title: American Nightmare: Trump's Breadline Kids
programme.desc: Ahead of the US elections, this documentary follows three children and their families in the battleground state of Ohio, where the Covid-19 pandemic has intensified their struggle to stay afloat. programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the medieval history of Lincoln, visiting the cathedral that was once the tallest building in the world and re-living the battle at Lincoln Castle. She discovers how moving up the social ladder in medieval times required a whole new wardrobe of clothing and accessories and gets top grips with a historically accurate crossbow. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson uses the latest drone technology to look at the geographical features that shaped the city's development

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Northampton, fitness instructor Pam and her boyfriend Glenn kick off the competition with a Spanish fiesta. programme.desc: Alex Horne sets another selection of inventively pointless tasks for the celebrity quintet of Daisy May Cooper, Johnny Vegas, Katherine Parkinson, Mawaan Rizwan and Richard Herring. Daisy flirts with a security guard, Richard is surprised by some chickens and Johnny convinces Alex to wear sparkly shoes while operating heavy machinery. As ever, Greg Davies decides who is the best of the worst

programme.title: Nazi Treasure Hunters
programme.desc: The treasure-hunting team travels to Germany, Holland, the USA, Spain and Switzerland to investigate Hermann Goering's massive multi-country art-looting operation, as they identify and track treasures that are still missing today

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody: The Home County Cartel
programme.desc: Part one of two. The documentary focuses on the importation of huge quantities of Class A drugs, following the work of covert detectives from the Eastern Region Special Operation Unit. A consignment weighing more than 100kg has been intercepted while being imported into the UK from Europe. To reveal who is behind this, an undercover operation is authorised to closely monitor the shipment, using audio and visual equipment, as it travels to its final destination at a farm in rural Hertfordshire

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody: The Home County Cartel
programme.desc: Part two of two. The covert team tries to find the true identity of Jaguar Palace, as well as the evidence to link the suspect directly to the drug-importing conspiracy. The detectives begin a nerve-shredding wait for the criminal network to set up its lucrative operation again, and stand ready to strike against the ringleaders when it does

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part one of two. Veteran actors and married couple Prunella Scales and Timothy West pass the tiller to Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth, who are celebrating 40 years of friendship by making new memories together and learning new skills. Sheila and Gyles learn the ropes with Tim, and set out by themselves along the upper reaches of the River Thames. It's the same river that roars past their homes in London, but just 50 miles upstream it's an Arcadian paradise of unspoiled nature, royal retreats and river regattas

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Back in 2017, George drafted in architect Chris Mason to help Wendy and Allan, whose Colchester home resembled a block of flats. They ended up with an enormous double-height extension and a Roman-style portico, but the ambitious scheme blew their budget and the project remained unfinished. George returns to find out whether they have managed to finish the property to the same exceptional standards

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates when Libby Parker is followed home by a gang of teenagers, and raped in the courtyard of her apartment block. Hundreds of windows overlooked the scene of the crime, but not one of the residents who witnessed the attack called the police. The prosecutors face an even more difficult dilemma when it emerges none of Libby's neighbours are willing to make a statement either. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hartigay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Notorious serial killer Gregory Williams Yeats concocts an elaborate plan to free himself from incarceration by co-operating with one-time nemesis Dr Carl Rudnick. When the pair ultimately break out of prison, Chicago detectives Lindsay and Dawson head to New York on the trail of fugitives, and the SVU team is enlisted to help bring the pair back to justice. The first of a two-part cross-over episode with Chicago PD, guest starring Sophia Bush and Jon Seda

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: John Drake, known to the team as career criminal Johnny D, faces trial for sex trafficking, rape, assault and kidnapping. However, as ADA Barba shores up the state's case against Drake, Benson's adoption of baby Noah - Drake's biological son - becomes entangled in the legal proceedings. As an intense courtroom showdown gets under way, the SVU faces a threat that could change the face of the team for ever. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hartigay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team is forced to return to an old crime scene, and when a case is taken to court, blackmail and deception lead to dramatic events

programme.title: The Enemy Within
programme.desc: After a bomb detonates in Jersey City, the team captures a planner who can take them to Tal, the mastermind behind the bombing, leading to a mission to stop another armed device

Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Tyne Daly, Greg Evigan and Don S Davis

programme.title: Columbo: A Friend in Deed (1974)
programme.desc: A Californian police commissioner helps a rich, influential friend to conceal his complicity in murdering his own wife - but reckons without detective Columbo's timely intervention and his dogged determination to bring the truth to light. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Richard Kiley and Rosemary Murphy

programme.title: Columbo: Murder with Too Many Notes (2000)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning composer Findlay Crawford relies on his assistant Gabriel to do all the hard work, but the younger musician demands recognition. To protect his reputation, Findlay kills Gabriel with a time-delayed drug to give himself the perfect alibi - but the slovenly sleuth sees through his ruse. Detective drama, with Peter Falk and Billy Connolly

programme.title: Columbo: Blueprint for Murder (1972)
programme.desc: An architect kills his business partner, and comes up with an elaborate scheme to make Columbo first suspect him, then reject him as the murderer. The villain begins by teasing the detective with hints on where he has hidden the body - hoping that when Columbo fails to find a corpse, he will look elsewhere for a killer. Crime drama, directed by and starring Peter Falk. With Patrick O'Neal and Janis Paige

programme.title: Columbo: Suitable for Framing (1971)
programme.desc: A millionaire art collector is thought to have been killed as the result of a random burglary. However, Columbo is convinced the dead man's nephew committed the crime to avoid being written out of the will, and embarks on a mission to ensnare his suspect. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Ross Martin and Don Ameche

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Crucial Game (1972)
programme.desc: The owner of an American football team is found dead in a swimming pool, and his drowning is regarded as an accident. The down-at-heel detective is convinced that foul play was involved and sets out to prove his case - but his chief suspect has a cast-iron alibi that places him miles away from the scene of the crime. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk and Robert Culp

programme.title: Columbo: The Greenhouse Jungle (1972)
programme.desc: A penniless rare-plant collector, desperate for funds to support his expensive hobby, fakes his nephew's kidnapping to get his hands on a trust fund. However, the supposed victim turns up dead, prompting Columbo to take action to unmask the killer. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Bradford Dillman and Ray Milland

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames examine the murder of a mother-of-two. The investigation leads them to a counterfeiting operation and the home of an actor - who puts them on the trail of an important official document. Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe star

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Rollins leads a sting operation to take down child pornographer Teddy Hawkins, also well-known as the owner of a chain of successful restaurants. However, when the team examines Teddy's cyber credentials, they discover he is connected to Deputy Commissioner Abraham, who vehemently denies any involvement in Hawkins' illicit distribution ring. With two high-profile arrests to their name, Rollins and her colleagues must work quickly to justify their actions before Abraham uses his power to make the charges 'go away'. Crime drama, starring Kelli Giddish

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A rape charge Barba is bringing against a popular actor is placed in jeopardy, prompting Rollins to step in and take matters into her own hands. Crime drama, starring Raul Esparza and Kelli Giddish

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Barba faces off against two ruthless defence attorneys who will stop at nothing to protect Yates

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team is forced to negotiate a difficult case when a teenager with gender identity issues is attacked by three boys. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: The Enemy Within
programme.desc: When an NSA analyst is killed in a car crash, Keaton and his team realise that she has stolen a piece of cloaking software that she intends to pass on to Tal. Shepherd decodes the encrypted message that Tal had left for the analyst, which reveals exactly where it was supposed to be delivered. Seizing their opportunity, Keaton asks Shepherd to go undercover, posing as the now dead analyst. Shepherd meets with Tal's contact, a young man called Braden. After a successful handover, the team learn that Tal is definitely in the US. In Washington DC, Tal meets with a mystery man who assures him that he can help with his FBI problem

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed by a stolen surveillance drone and the team links it to elusive terrorist Benham Parsa. NCIS joins forces with the Department of Defence to track down the device before it is used for a large-scale attack, but Gibbs is less than pleased that he has to work with his ex-lover Hollis Mann. Meanwhile, McGee hopes he will be granted time off to attend a black-tie gala honouring his girlfriend Delilah. Guest starring Susanna Thompson (Arrow)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.desc: The events that led Lorraine Thorpe to become Britain's youngest female double murderer at the age of 15. She and 41-year-old Paul Clarke tortured and killed their friend Rosalyn Hunt, before smothering Thorpe's father Desmond to death with a cushion after he threatened to go to the police. The programme features interviews with Rosalyn's brother as well as detectives from the joint Norfolk and Suffolk investigation team

programme.title: Spitfire Women
programme.desc: The story of the 168 female pilots who served in Britain's Air Transport Auxiliary - an organisation responsible for delivering aircraft to the RAF on the front lines of battle during the Second World War. Despite having no radio or navigation equipment, ATA pilots were expected to fly any kind of aircraft to the destinations where they were most needed, often risking their lives in the process. The film also features interviews with the last surviving female veterans, who speak about the dangers they encountered in the skies and the discrimination they faced back home

programme.title: Piccadilly Incident (1946)
programme.desc: A Wren is pronounced missing in action, but returns three years later to find her husband has remarried. Melodrama, starring Anna Neagle and Michael Wilding

programme.title: Forty Thousand Horsemen (1941)
programme.desc: Three Australian volunteers take part in the last cavalry charge in modern warfare while serving in Palestine. First World War drama, starring Grant Taylor and Betty Bryant

programme.title: The Big Blockade (1942)
programme.desc: Second World War mock-documentary, featuring a series of scenes about the importance of Britain's economic blockade on Germany, demonstrating the international repercussions of the action and the responses of the British public. Starring Leslie Banks, Michael Redgrave, John Mills, Will Hay and Robert Morley

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra

programme.title: Spitfire Women
programme.desc: The story of the 168 female pilots who served in Britain's Air Transport Auxiliary - an organisation responsible for delivering aircraft to the RAF on the front lines of battle during the Second World War. Despite having no radio or navigation equipment, ATA pilots were expected to fly any kind of aircraft to the destinations where they were most needed, often risking their lives in the process. The film also features interviews with the last surviving female veterans, who speak about the dangers they encountered in the skies and the discrimination they faced back home

programme.title: Death or Freedom: The Jericho Jailbreak
programme.desc: The story of Operation Jericho, a 1944 mission that saw a squadron of RAF Mosquito bombers try to free French Resistance fighters from a prison in Amiens

programme.title: Dam Busters
programme.desc: The stories behind one of the most daring raids of the Second World War, in which a squadron of Lancaster bombers destroyed two dams in the heart of industrial Germany

programme.title: The Last Day of WW1
programme.desc: An account of how soldiers continued to be killed in battle hours after the armistice between the Allies and Germany had been signed in 1918. Presented by Michael Palin

programme.title: Churchill's Traitors
programme.desc: An in-depth look at those who betrayed Winston Churchill during the Second World War, and the events that led to the attack on Pearl Harbour in December 1941

programme.title: The Loft (2014)
programme.desc: Five successful married men rent an apartment where they can carry out their extra-marital affairs in secret. The arrangement works well until they find the corpse of a beautiful woman handcuffed to a bed - but which of them is the killer, and how will the grisly discovery affect their friendship? Thriller, with Karl Urban, James Marsden and Wentworth Miller

programme.title: The Escort (2015)
programme.desc: A sex-obsessed journalist whose career is falling apart has a chance encounter in a bar with a high-class prostitute. Seeing a chance to write the story that will make him a success, he persuades her to let him document her life, and a strange connection develops between the two lonely outsiders. Comedy drama, starring Michael Doneger, Lyndsy Fonseca and Bruce Campbell

programme.title: Cruel Passion (1977)
programme.desc: Two sisters are expelled from a nunnery and are plunged into a life of debauchery. While one embraces her new lifestyle, the other is desperate to cling to her old morality and sees a hope of salvation in a mysterious aristocrat who harbours a hidden dark side. Historical drama based on the novel Justine by the Marquis de Sade, starring Koo Stark and Martin Potter

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny follows a woman who plans to live with her family in the property she wants to develop, and keep a close eye on her £400,000 dream. But as the project progresses, she loses control of both the development and the budget, putting the entire undertaking at risk of failure

programme.title: A Place by the Sea

programme.title: Angels One Five (1953)
programme.desc: A new recruit joins a squadron of British fighter pilots in the summer of 1940, as they prepare to face a confrontation with the overwhelming forces of the Luftwaffe over the English Channel, while the ground crew at their base anxiously awaits reports from the conflict. Second World War adventure, starring Jack Hawkins and John Gregson

programme.title: Date with a Dream (1948)
programme.desc: Four wartime performers well known for their rousing concert recitals decide to reform and become a successful nightclub act all over again. Upbeat comedy, starring Wally Patch, Len Lowe, Terry-Thomas, Norman Wisdom and Jean Carson

programme.title: The High Bright Sun (1965)
programme.desc: A military officer in 1950s Cyprus tries to negotiate a truce with a partisan leader during an uprising against the British occupation. Drama, with Dirk Bogarde and George Chakiris

programme.title: I Was a Spy (1933)
programme.desc: A Belgian nurse infiltrates German High Command to supply the British with vital information during the First World War One. Fact-based spy thriller, starring Madeleine Carroll, Conrad Veidt, Donald Calthorp and Nigel Bruce

programme.title: Dam Busters
programme.desc: The stories behind one of the most daring raids of the Second World War, in which a squadron of Lancaster bombers destroyed two dams in the heart of industrial Germany

programme.title: Blitz Spirit (2019)
programme.desc: Short film set during the London Blitz, in which a pianist struggles to justify his war efforts to his stubborn mother

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams looks at the role - and the unusual odd jobs - of country policemen in the South and South East during the Second World War, when rumours of chicken stealing and illegal pig roasts were fairly common diversions from the privations of rationing

programme.title: The Last Day of WW1
programme.desc: An account of how soldiers continued to be killed in battle hours after the armistice between the Allies and Germany had been signed in 1918. Presented by Michael Palin

programme.title: Harry Patch - The Last Tommy
programme.desc: Harry Patch, Britain's longest-surviving First World War soldier, joins with five other veterans in reflecting on their lives after the 1918 Armistice. Patch, who died in 2009 at the age of 111, recalls the horrors of trench warfare and how he became a passionate advocate for peace in his later years

programme.title: Churchill's First World War
programme.desc: Drama-documentary charting Winston Churchill's road to redemption after his disgrace following the Dardanelles disaster during the First World War through intimate letters he wrote to his wife Clementine. It reveals how he was instrumental in the creation of the tank and notes his contribution to the Allied victory in 1918 as Minister of Munitions, demonstrating how the future prime minister was shaped by his experiences of the Great War

programme.title: Operation Chromite (2016)
programme.desc: A squad of South Korean soldiers sent to infiltrate their northern neighbours during the crucial Battle of Incheon. Wartime drama, starring Lee Jung-jae and Liam Neeson

programme.title: The Bionic Vet

Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Eric Portman, Reginald Purdell and Niall MacGinnis

The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: FA Cup Classics

programme.title: FA Cup Classics
programme.desc: We revisit the thrilling 1997 FA Cup fourth round tie between Chelsea and league leaders Liverpool at Stamford Bridge. It had been 27 years since Chelsea last lifted the trophy

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Watford. Action from the 2012/13 Championship play-off final at Wembley Stadium, as the teams contested a place in the top flight alongside the already promoted Cardiff City and Hull City. Palace reached this match when Wilfried Zaha scored a brace in a 2-0 away win against Brighton & Hove Albion after a goalless draw in the first leg. Meanwhile, Watford bounced back from narrowly missing out on automatic promotion by overcoming Leicester City in dramatic fashion, with Troy Deeney scoring an injury time-winner on the counter after Manuel Almunia had saved a penalty

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Brentford v Yeovil Town. Action from the 2012/13 League One play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where the remaining place in the following season's Championship was decided. Brentford agonisingly missed out on automatic promotion on the final day of the regular campaign, but recovered to defeat Swindon Town on penalties in their semi-final, while Yeovil were 2-1 aggregate victors over Sheffield United

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Northampton Town. Action from the 2012/13 League Two play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where promotion to League One awaited the winners. The Bantams were making their second appearance of the season at Wembley having also reached the League Cup final following an outstanding giant-killing run, and they needed to come from behind to edge out Burton Albion to reach this stage. The Cobblers ended a run of recent poor form to win 1-0 in both legs of their semi-final against Cheltenham Town to move within one match of reaching the third tier of English football

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Swansea City. Highlights from Wembley Stadium in 2013, where the League Two Bantams were aiming to produce yet another cup upset. Swansea were appearing in their first major cup final and beat Chelsea in the semi-finals to reach this stage, but Bradford had stolen most of the headlines in the competition this season, belying their lowly league status to eliminate the likes of Arsenal, Aston Villa and Wigan Athletic

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur. The showpiece match in English football's second cup competition at Wembley Stadium

They also became the first team to win the showpiece match three times in four years
